### Post by bored2k on 2005-06-28
[CENTER][QUOTE=Wikipedia]In English, an otaku (plural usually otaku, since Japanese words are not pluralized using an "s") is a variety of geek (or an overly obsessed fanboy / fangirl) specializing in anime and manga. A simplistic English translation might be 'culture hermit'.[/QUOTE] [/CENTER] 

[Sapo](http://www.ubuntuforums.org/member.php?userid=12926)  recommended it, here it is.

Personally, I **[COLOR=Red]*h*eart[/COLOR]** anime. I don't get to watch as much as I'd want to, but boy do I love it. It helps me get away from whatever is keeping me busy (mainly College ...). I love the way they boldly express things (big eyes .rock) and the whole I don't give a [Spock](http://conal.net/pan/Gallery/Old/spock-out.gif) attitude.

So once more, let's use this thread to talk about our beloved anime (or hated, if that's you). And yet again, anime diverts my attention :D.

[CENTER][[IMG]http://img9.echo.cx/img9/5263/lain38003gb.jpg[/IMG]](http://66.111.49.71/wallpapers/lain/lain_3_800.jpg)[/CENTER]

### Post by kvidell on 2005-06-28
However in Japanese it means "social outcast" or "one with no life" (vaguely... I think it more fully means someone who is so caught up in something other, culturaly more important, parts of their life start to fail.. or they become a hermit completely. Not a good thing to call someone either way.)

I like a few animes...
Neon Genesis, Trigun, Hellsing... er... yea, I think that's it.

### Post by bored2k on 2005-06-28
[QUOTE=sapo]I think we could all start posting the animes we got.. so if anyone get interested he could ask if its good or not.. here are my little colletion.. (i hope i remember everything)
[/QUOTE]

Well I recently wiped off my entire hard drive, keeping no backups at all, so I don't have anything other than the current Bleach episodes (the ONLY thing I miss is my Cowboy Bebop, that somehow I forgot until it was too late !).

But yeah, great way to ask for personal reviews. Sapo you have some animes I have wanted to acquire recently, but was a little scheptical, so I'll start off with you mate :D .

- Samurai Champloo. Some people love it, some people say it's just a PG rated not so good version of Rurouni Kenshin (no making fun of Battousai.. or else). Tell us what it really is :D. 

- Full Metal ALchemist. I guess animenfo didn't really convince me. An alchemist that does what ? WHy is it so cool ?

- Hunter X Hunter. I have read that this one rocks, but I saw a glimpse of an episode and wasn't really turned on. ?

P.S. - Man, I have the phrase "So long, space cowboy" printed on my forehead.. I just can't forget it. I don't know why.  Maybe it was because I watched Bebop in a single weekend (*So* got hooked). Some episodes were really touchy (last episodes looked a lot like Kenshin's last Ova were his girl dies) .. some really kick ass (like the 1st time we see Spike's sweet fighting foot work against the big tall black dude), but they all rock.

[Off-Topic] Yeah I just noticed myself. I don't really remember ever seeing teh "100% ubuntu" rank though..[/Off-Topic]

### Post by Quest-Master on 2005-06-28
Here I go!

SAMURAI CHAMPLOO: Completed -- Coming from the guy who made Bebop, this anime screamed of an awesome style and was just plain cool. The first episode was probably one of the best, though some after were a bit boring. Still quite nice though.

GUNDAM SEED DESTINY: 36, really interesting, and haven't seen the old Gundam so it isn't repetetive or anything, at least for me. I can't wait for the next Saturday to come as soon as I finish an episode.

GANKUTSUOU: Completed -- An anime version of the Count of Monte Cristo.. pretty suspenseful and weird. Took a different approach on textures too.

BLEACH: 36, what can I say? A few cliches, sometimes cheesy, but just so damn fun to watch. It's just great. <3

FULL METAL ALCHEMIST: Completed -- Best anime ever created IMO. I absolutely loved this. Wonderful storyline, great characters, and a terrific ending. I believe this is a must-watch for any otaku.

GUNDAM SEED: Completed -- The preface to SEED Destiny. Another great series, very suspenseful and should keep you hooked and satisfied till the ending.

NARUTO: 103 -- What otaku hasn't seen this? Some complain of it's cliches and cheesyness, but this one, like Bleach, is just so much fun to watch. I really enjoy getting to the next Naruto episode. Lovely fights, great characters, and a suspenseful storyline too.

PARANOIA AGENT: Completed -- You can't be an otaku and not watch this. Created by the guy who did the Lain series, you KNOW this is going to be one hell of a ride. Incredibly weird, and will keep you mind-boggled endlessly till you reach the awesome ending.

COWBOY BEBOP: Completed -- An incredibly stylish and fun anime. You can't miss this one.

SPEED GRAPHER: 10 -- One of the most promising animes of the new season. A new take on characters and a plot, and suspenseful as well. You'll really like this one, no matter what. It's just plain cool.

WOLFS RAIN: 11 -- I picked this up as I hadn't watched it earlier, and it's very nice. Great music by Yoko Kanno, and a great plot. :)

GUNDAM WING: Completed -- One of the first animes I watched. Very memorable, and probably the frontman of the entire Gundam series. Fantastic artwork, and a well-crafted story makes this a joy.

Phew. I think there are probably more I haven't listed, hehe.

### Post by psychicdragon on 2005-06-28
I'm obviously not as big an otaku as some, looks at sapo  :smile: 
But recently I have been watching a bit of anime again which I havent done in a while.

Cowboy Bebop holds a special place in my heart. It's what got me interested in anime again. Everything about the series just exudes syle, the music is beautiful (composed by Yoko Kanno), top notch animation, and great plot points. I think it's probably the best piece of tv science-fiction I've ever seen, there were some really good classic scifi kind of stories. The way the main plot was sort of hidden and then all came together in the end was so beautiful and bitter-sweet. The final 6 episodes or so were all pretty dark.

I watched Samurai Champloo a couple weeks ago. It was pretty good but just couldn't compare to Bebop for me. The conclusion to the first season was so good though, I loved those two episodes. It seemed to me that Mugen was pretty much exactly the same character as Spike, anyone else notice the similarities?

Anyone else a fan of Miyazaki's work? The Castle of Cagliostro, Spirited Away, and Nausicaa are all some of my very favorite movies ever. I still haven't seen Howl's Flying Castle though  :-x 

I totally love One Peice as well.

EDIT: Since no one has mentioned My HiME (Mai HiME) yet. It's a fun series kind of in the vein of Sailor Moon (magic girls!). The story builds really well and there are some pretty cool fight scenes. The ending unfortunately wasn't the best.

### Post by bored2k on 2005-06-28
> Shortly after the September 11, 2001 attacks, Cartoon Network decided not to air episode 22, Cowboy Funk, which featured a terrorist who blew up tall buildings with bombs. After some time, the episode was eventually put back in the regular sequence of episodes. The real-life terror attacks and subsequent anthrax scare were also credited with delaying the release of the Cowboy Bebop movie in the United States by Sony Pictures, which featured a bounty-head who used technobiological terrorism.

Following the Space Shuttle Columbia disaster in 2003, Cartoon Network also decided not to air episode 19, Wild Horses, in which the Columbia shuttle was featured as a prominent plot point in the story. The episode had been shown in previous airings of the series, and has since been put back into the rotation.Just read that from the Wiki. That's bizarre :s

### Post by bored2k on 2005-06-29
> there's nothing better than Japanese original anime with subs...  **Exactly.** I personally like watching films and anime in their native languages. I mean, your brazilian (I'm hispanic - Dominican), you and I have seen some dubbed movies, and they're all wrong and just pityful. English films were made to be seen in english language, not some horrid Spanish translation *lol*. Imagine *Cidade de Deus* or *Maria Full Of Grace* in English, it would totally loose the mood. Some translations are really good, like Kenshin's spanish one (VERY good), but others sound all blocky and "japanese action flick translated to english" cheap.

> Naruto is a bloody hell anime.. they are gonna just keep the chats or what? True. They always have an excuse to trim down certain politically incorrect aspects for the American versions.

### Post by NeoChaosX on 2005-06-29
Oh god, I love wathcing anime. Last year or so I've been introduced to quite a bit of it, and I'll share some thoughts on what I've seen so far.

Fullmetal Alchemist: This series is AMAZING. Love all the twists and turns it took throughout it's 51 episodes (and can't wait for the movie that concludes the series to come out in English). Animation is pretty much top-notch throught the series. I also watched the English episodes on Cartoon Network, and I'm amazed at how good the voice work is.  :grin:  And Al is the most sympathetic character in the series, if not in all of anime.

Naruto: I've spent the last couple of months catching up to both the manga and anime for this, and I'm loving it. It reminds me a bit of Dragon Ball Z (with ninjas instead of martial artists), but that's a good thing, as I liked DBZ! I don't like how the anime deviates from the manga by adding extra stories, but it's not too bad of a series. Excellent fights, and interesting stories abound. Hell, my Ubuntu desktop even has a background with Hinata (my favorite character from the series, btw) on it! (Anybody got a Hinata skin I can use for XMMS to match?)

---

### Post by darkoptix on 2005-06-29
Anime rules... Ever since I was watching Dragon Ball Z and thinking to myself anime sucks, until I heard of the almightly Ghost in the Shell and Akira. These two movies bought me over to the world of anime. Here is a list of some of my favs so far from me watching.(I'm on dialup btw, so downloading is bloody hard).
Movies(watched and loved):
Ghost in the Shell (scifi triller)
Ghost in the Shell 2 ( Great animation and story )
Cowboy Bebop ( Great movie that adds to bebop)
Akira ( OMFG this movie is great... i'm reading the comics right now )
Ninja Scroll ( Samurai Fun )
Appleseed ( another gits-like movie )
Princess Mononoke(alright)

Series(watched and loved):
Cowboy Bebop ( Most original show i've seen to date )
Neon Genesis Evangelion ( one of my most fav shows of all time )
Naruto (only alright in my opinion... or not that great...)
Samurai Champloo ( ranks with cowboy bebop )
Full Metal Panic ( Great Show... cannot wait for the second raid )
Rahzephon ( good show that is similar to evangelion )
Full Metal Alchemist ( have and am on ep 5... so far so good ) 
Last Exile ( steam punk owns joo )

Here are some shows I have wanted to see, but since my anime source has seen on all on his illegal cartoon network(in canada it's illegal) and does not want to download... I have to wait till I move out and get broadband.
Movie and series I want to see:
Perfect Blue
Ghost in the Shell: SAC
Noir 
plus others i cannot think of right now...

I also enjoy watching anime with subs in the original language... It's seems more real. However, I also liked halo 2 when I had it in french before the release... the aliens don't speak english  :-? ...

### Post by Bo Rosén on 2005-06-29
Hi,
I'm a newbie when it comes to anime so I am completely cofused about everything that's available. I have seen several of Miyazaki's movies and enjoyed them all for different reasons. I've seen Neon Genesis Evangelion and thought it was pretty good, but nothing terrific. I've fallen completely for Chobits, even if I'm probably not its target audience :grin:  It's a nice mix of teen romance, humour, mystery and computer/AI - human relations, a topic I find very interesting.

The first episode of Full Metal Alchemist didn't persuade me, but I'll give it a few more tries. I got Cowboy Bebop because I heard it was in some ways reminicent of Firefly and it looks promising. 
Flame of Recca - nah not after the first ep. Just doesn't look very interesting and the main character is irritating.

So, it seems I like (low key) humour, a little romance, mystery, interesting characters and a good plot. Not terribly interested in blood and fights, but certainly don't mind as long as they bring the story forward.

Suggestions?

---

### Post by Kyral on 2005-07-02
Wow, this thread came up while i was away, SHAME ON YOU!

Anyway, here goes my anime collection :D

(Preface: I'm a Gundam Fanboy :D)

Mobile Suit Gundam The 08th MS Team and Miller's Report: Really quick 12 episode OVA set during the One Year War (Miller's Report is a "movie" and was incorporated into episode 8 when it was aired on CN, quite well too). Basically a love story. First anime I ever bought.

Mobile Fighter G Gundam: Quirky, not standard Gundam fare, fun all around. Nice underlying love story.

After War Gundam X: Canceled after about 36 episodes, but still shines as one of my favorite series of all time. Definately worth checking out, even for non Gundam fanboys.

Mobile Suit Gundam 0080: The War In The Pocket: 6 episode OVA, tearjerker.

Mobile Suit Gundam 0083: Stardust Memories: Love it or hate it, it fulfils its role as a  bridge between Mobile Suit Gundam and Mobile Suit Zeta Gundam.

Mobile Suit Gundam: The original. Classic :D

Mobile Suit Zeta Gundam: Considered by many to be the best of all Gundam. Serious war drama.

Mobile Suit Gundam Double Zeta: A second season to Zeta, first half is much lighter compared to Zeta, but the second half brings the old feeling of Zeta back.

Mobile Suit Gundam Char's Counterattack: An end to the Char - Amuro rivalry. Again, quite like Zeta.

Mobile Suit Gundam F91: A feature length movie, starts off the Crossbone "saga".

Mobile Suit Victory Gundam: A product of Tomino's depression, very dark, yet entertaining.

New Mobile Suit Report Gundam Wing: The Gundam that most casual anime fans in America prolly know best, ranks down in my list :D

Mobile Suit Turn-A Gundam: Tomino's only entry into the Alternate Universe, much more upbeat than Victory. Origin of the "Turn-A Bang" theory.

Mobile Suit Gundam SEED and Destiny: The latest entries into the Gundam saga. Seem to be warning us about the possible future of genetic engineering. Uses many Gundam cliches, but I do quite enjoy it.

G-Savior: Live action movie. Would have been good if it hadn't had the Gundam moniker attached, but it had so much to live up to and couldn't.

Okay, thats it for Gundam :P Now for my other stuff.

Full Metal Panic: Combine Gundam and Ah! My Goddess and this is what you get. Enough mecha action to keep Mecha diehards pleased and the romantic comedy intertwined is quite funny. Plus Sagara makes me look **good**.

Full Metal Panic Fomuffu: Like FMP, but almost no mecha action. But still romantic comedy goodness with Sagara's military mishaps :D

Cowboy Bebop: Do I really need to say anything?

Furi Kuri (FLCL): Insane doesn't begin to describe it. The producers were on crack when they wrote this.

Ah! My Goddess: Cute, funny, and a great romantic comedy.

Macross 7: Love it or hate it, watching Basara is just so stupid its funny.

Rurouni Kenshin: The classic samurai anime. The Kyoto Arc is one of the best parts of the series.

Samurai Champloo: What I would call a stylized and updated version of Kenshin, but still very nice :D


So there you have it :D

### Post by Kyral on 2005-07-04
Okay let me tell you what makes it cool.

You have the Kenshinish character, Jin, with his own dark past, but not as light hearted (kinda like Battosai a lot).

Then you have Mugan, who in my own words looks like if you took a street punk from NYC (no offense) and dropped him into Edo Japan and gave him mad crazy sword skills.

Then you have Fuu, who is like Kaoru, but younger.

The series rocks on its own, has Kenshin like elements, but isn't Kenshin.

Oh, and Battlecry is one of THE BEST openers EVER.

Yanno what, just go watch it :D

### Post by bored2k on 2005-07-10
So out of boredom, I'll review the anime I saw today:

1. Bleach episode 39.
One word: wow. Right now Kenpachi is one of the coolest villains I can remember of ! (for some reason, his face/style reminds me of Shishio from Rurouni Kenshin). Ichigo's sword side story is really getting in shape now into an interesting story on its own :D. Also, the episode had a nice comic relief on the guy with the Fro' ^_^. Last but not least, Kenpachi's lieutenant (the one on his shoulder) is mad cute even when she's mad :=)

2. Fullmetal Alchemist episodes 21-22.
This series has got me intrigued from the beggining. I won't stress much on how much I'm loving it, but I will say this: episode 23 was yet another eye opener (specially after they showed a myriad  of villains I still don't know off :-o).

3. Samurai Champloo episode 1.
After seeing how the last FMA episode I saw kind of like gave a whole new life to the anime, I decided to pause it a bit so I could finally see what Champloo is all about. After watching the first episode, It felt like a complete déjà vu. I totally felt like I was watching Bebop in the Edo era.  I was impressed with FMA visuals, but Champloo's visuals are just way too hip and awsome.

It's kind of hard to see these three anime beasts simulatenously, but they're all graet :D !

> LOS ANGELES (April 6, 2005) - New Line Cinema has acquired the feature film rights to "Monster," the fastest-selling Japanese manga comic book series ever, from legendary author Naoki Urasawa.

"Monster" is a fast-paced psychological thriller that tells the story of a young doctor who saves the life of a little boy - only to find a few years later that the life he saved was that of a brilliant killer. The doctor must then track down the young killer and solve a larger conspiracy.

yo bored.. you said that you was going to start learning japanease.. i ve started this week to learn hiragana... i ve bought a book and i m printing a lot of worksheets from online materials.. i ve found a lot of good websites and i m alread understanding some hiragana stuff.. here some links:

A lot of good free materials:
[http://www.mlcjapanese.co.jp/Download.htm](http://www.mlcjapanese.co.jp/Download.htm)

A lot of links:
[http://www.grandgent.com/tom/j/](http://www.grandgent.com/tom/j/)

Some gloassary stuff:
[http://www.uiowa.edu/~japanese/NAKAMA/](http://www.uiowa.edu/~japanese/NAKAMA/)

A RPG to learn Katakana (has a linux version, i m playing with it hehe)
[http://lrnj.com/](http://lrnj.com/)

A good dictionary with a lot of examples:
[http://www.mahou.org/Dict/](http://www.mahou.org/Dict/)

English - Japanese - English Dict:
[http://www.freedict.com/onldict/jap.html](http://www.freedict.com/onldict/jap.html)

hehe i m learning a lot of stuff using anime as a reference.. like this:

```

shinigami =  &#12375;&#12395;&#12364;&#12415;&#12288;death god
Shi = &#12375;
ni = &#12395;
ga = &#12364;
mi = &#12415;

```

thats not that difficult as peoples say.. and the pronunciation is the same as portuguese.. so it is even easier  :grin:

### Post by bored2k on 2005-07-17
[Center][/Center]Center topic: Midway entertainment

**Act 1 - Farewell Ceremony**
Fullmetal Alchemist: 25/51 (49%)
  I'm finally halfway the Fullmetal Alchemist series. I have to say, if the series ended right now, it would still be in my top 5 -if not on top- favorite anime list. It's so rich from every possible aspect. It's comedy _really_ makes me laugh, the action scenes keep me on the edge of my seat, character development and involvement is superb and the dramatic scenes make me feel melancholic :-X (Brigadier Hughes on episode 25.. man that was though) plus several other aspects. Although I can't wait to see the other half, I'm just as lost as to what is going to happen, wich is happening to me also in Monster: after 9 episodes, I have no idea where this series is going O.o. But still, me and my buddies enjoyed our little Monster session ^_^.

**Act 2 - Edo Period Bohemian Rhapsody**
Samurai Champloo: 09/26 (34.6%)
  So far, Champloo's fresh and good, but not _that_ good. Animation is realistic beyond belief
and very sweet and well done (although they're so realistic looking that character's can't be as emotive as I'd like them to be -I can still see Mugen's crazy though). Like I was warned by the theOtaku.com's review (I love their reviews), it tends to divert quite a bit with no apparent sign of certain plot twists having any effect nor connection with their ultimate goal (sorry for making *comparisons*, but this distracting divertion is something I've yet to experience in FMA). Like I was also warned, fight scenes tend to be rather short :? and not half as enganging as they brag about prior to fighting (*comparison:* would you even imagine a 30 second "that's it" battle between Kenshin and Saito O.o ?). On the other hand, the easy sailing, bohemian mood the series uses is really relaxing and entertaining.

**Act 3 - Post-traumatic stress disorder**
Monster: 09/78 (11.5%)
  What the heck did _we_ get ourselves into? I started watching this one on Friday night with a couple of buddies and we were *"Wowed"* _several_ times in such a few episodes. Personally, I dislike the animation (someone could have told me this was a 12 year old anime and I would have probably believed the person) but this series is about everything but trying to be nice to the viewer/reader. Right now we are in a state of .. I'll quote some of the things we said throught or 09 viewed episodes
- Episode 01-03: "That _leech_ ! Someone slap her!" ; "What kind of hospital is that O.o?!" ; "Are they really like that :-S.." "You see that psycho typist/investigator? We are now calling him The Hacker" ... "Wow." 
- "WHERE is this going ?! And what MORE could they add in 70+ chapters ?!" "Dude.. the anime could have ended up right now and I would have been satisfied."
- Episode 07-X: "Plot twist. Wow"

This anime will probably take me the longest to see as I pretend to see it when my buddies are with me (not that I'm scared(!), but we want to watch it together.. not like we have a choice, we're helping ourselves with the big downloads, so one needs the other).

**Act 4 - Shounen Rejoice!**
Bleach: 40/XXX (X%)
This one's good man. All of us could mention several things we don't like about it (hint: Useless Side Stories in the middle of "dinner"), but let's face it, once the episode's over, we can't wait for the next one (this is way I think following a weekly series is almost like self-inflicted torture). Besides the whole kick ass Kenpachi battle (talk about an intriguing battle), the thing that has me intrigues the most is how are the other characters besides Ichigo going to square against the other Captains. This one looks like it's not going to end anytime soon so I'm really glad I picked up while It still wasn't _too_ big (hint: Naruto.. I will get you some day.. grr). I like the fact that I like to think that besides Hollows, there are no real bad guys (at least in general). From a perspective, Ichigo and crew would even look like the bad guys (hey, they were the ones who erupted into the Shinigami's world). Apparently, we'll have to keep an eye out for this one for months to come.
--

Anyways, that was my half and not-really-half review of the anime. I know it could be not really accurate at some points, but that's my perception of what I am seeing so far. Of course, I will re-post once I'm done/more advanced with them.

---

### Post by sapo on 2005-07-17
[QUOTE=bored2k][Center][/Center]Center topic: Midway entertainment

**Act 1 - Farewell Ceremony**
Fullmetal Alchemist: 25/51 (49%)
  I'm finally halfway the Fullmetal Alchemist series. I have to say, if the series ended right now, it would still be in my top 5 -if not on top- favorite anime list. It's so rich from every possible aspect. It's comedy _really_ makes me laugh, the action scenes keep me on the edge of my seat, character development and involvement is superb and the dramatic scenes make me feel melancholic :-X (Brigadier Hughes on episode 25.. man that was though) plus several other aspects. 1. Is Naruto over ? If not, does it show any signs of ending anytime soon ? How many chapters are there so far (last time I checked, I would have had to download approximately 25GB :-/)

2. Yes, "some" side stories are boring (so far, the dutch guy that ate a lot, the lame assassin, the goblins et al have not really been that fun). Right now the thing attaching me to this anime is the three main characters. I like Mugen's wacky and comedic way, Jin's "serious" yet permissive style (a samurai that sells his sword in a pawn shop?!) and I generally fall for cute characters like Fuu  :-# (like Ed's Automail chick and Kenpachi's Lieutenant.. they're so cute ^_^). Comedy here is good, but not even close to FMA's.

3. Plot characters dying? In Monster? That's not unexpected :-P. Heck, one of my brothers kept waiting for the "Damn Monster" to appear! (that is, until the lock picker calls the young "Dicaprio" *monster*- I think my brother was disappointed :?). Plot twists? they're always healthy.

4. Bleach is good man.. so good. It could be dubbed a simple brainless shounen and it could arguably be one, but I'm still so intrigued and attached by it. I know certain anime specialize in making the current enemy impossible to beat then making the next one even MORE impossible, but if Bleach pulls this out, this will be an anime I won't forget in a while -if ever. Ever since they showed Zaraki's powers I've been thinking (there's like a dozen other captains *minus* one plus the old man, and they don't really look half as kick ass and fierce as Kenpachi does, what the heck will this guys come up with? The only possibility I can think of is them acknowledging Ichigo's powers after 1 or 2 more battles and make him on of their team and release Kuchuki, but that would be way too expected for a series like Bleach ;). And by the way dude, what release did you see? In Lunar's late release, at the end when they show the trailers, you do see Ichigo swinging at Kuchuki's Captain ..

"]And bored... i hope i had some anime freak friend to watch anime with me.. but nobody here likes anime.. i just watch everything with my friend nina (my little kitty)   :roll:"

### Post by bored2k on 2005-07-19
Ok so now I've watched the first 16 episodes of Samurai Champloo wich means I've seen 61.5% of it :D. I must admit, the last 3 or 4 episodes have positively changed my mind about the series. It's still goofy and a little bland at times, but the plot of these last episodes have really lifted my perception of the series. I hope it keeps the "good episode!" streak going. After all, its just 26 episodes long, and FMA is twice and big and packs drama from minute 0 to minute 23.42 (and don't get me started on the 95 eps of Kenshin.. although by the end of FMA I'll have to think about wich one I liked the most).

I've paused _All_ my other anime until I finish with this one *or* until it falls to the -not as entertaining as the last episode- dark side again.

P.S. - From what I'm able to see, the fact that every episode is sort of a stand alone chapter in the series thus bringing new and unexpected things to the table (although this series revolves a whole lot around them being three bums starving to death) makes it have a pretty nice replay value (good for my newly started anime collection :D).

### Post by bored2k on 2005-07-20
[QUOTE=Kyral]I haven't heard anything about Samurai 7. What is it supposed to be like?

Oh, I started to download Bleach today. Putting it on my /home/anime dir b/c my /media/anime only has about 8 GB left and there are still series on there that need to be finished...[/QUOTE]
Have you seen Samurai Seven the OLD breakthrough movie ? [http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0047478/](http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0047478/)

Well it's a new take on the storyline, only blending it with mecha and well.. samurais. From what I've heard it has superb graphics, and being a tech updated version of the old movie, its supposed to be good, plus, its only about 26 episodes long :D.

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Samurai_7](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Samurai_7)
[http://animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=3127](http://animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=3127)
[http://www.animenfo.com/animetitle,1410,sazgub,samurai_7.html](http://www.animenfo.com/animetitle,1410,sazgub,samurai_7.html)

> Samurai 7 is an anime series based on Akira Kurosawa's classic 1954 movie entitled The Seven Samurai. The series is being directed by Toshifumi Takizawa and produced by Shouji Murahama. There are 26 episodes to this series with each episode costing around ¥32,500,000 ($300,000) to make. It has been announced that FUNimation will get the rights to dubbing this series.




Plot (no spoilers) 
The village of Kanna is a nice rice village with a problem. Every year during harvest time huge mechanical robots, known as the Nobuseri, come and steal away almost all of the rice that had been harvested that year. Not only that, the Nobuseri are becoming more demanding, now taking women and children in addition to rice. The elder of Kanna has declared that to protect the village they must hire samurai. However, they have no money and thus must find samurai willing to protect the village for the price of all the rice that he/she can eat. He sends three members of the village out to the city to begin looking. And thus the journey begins.

### Post by bored2k on 2005-07-20
So now I have finished watching **Samurai Champloo**. Scratch most of what I said, this one rocks. It is true, the main plot seems to be a somewhat vague specially if you notice all the bad things that happen because of it. But man oh man, what _a_ finale does this series rock ! Although fight scenes were really a lot sorter than what one would except, in substance they are beefy, well done and mostly unique. I particularly enjoyed the way the last chapters resembled Bebop a lot more than the others (with the über weird chapters like the Baseball one; the jazzy music in the last two and the cinematic feel of -we're doomed..no we're really doomed!-).

	Animation  10/10
	Sound	   9/10
	Creativity 10.5/10
	Main Plot  8.9/10 (the last episode really mad me forget how goofy it was)
	Other Plots 9/10
	Overall  9/10

### Post by bored2k on 2005-07-25
I read a little of FMA's manga today and I picked up this weird but nice variation:

On episode 36 of the anime, starting on minute 19, the _full-grown_ Winry and Riza start a conversation about Riza firing her gun at others at who she protects. On the manga, this same dialogue happens waay back to when Roy was recruiting the brothers shortly after the initial "incident", where we see the little Winry asking these questions to Riza, who instead of assembling her gun like the anime, is holding a cup of coffee (while Mustang is questioning the brothers on locked doors). Something interesting I found was the fact that on both situations Winry shows her dislike for Roy Mustang (Anime because he killed her parents; Manga because he's trying to take the brothers away from her).

[IMG]http://img299.imageshack.us/img299/2074/1307ck.jpg[/IMG][IMG]http://img227.imageshack.us/img227/5685/screenshot02ne.jpg[/IMG]

And yes, I'm enjoying this anime a _lot_ ;).

Edit 1: Another slight variation. In the anime, after Winry fixes Ed's automail, he starts sparring with Al for wich Winry tells them to stop because he'll break his automail that way. The exact same happens in the manga, but instead, it happens when Ed gets the automail installed in the first place. [http://groups.msn.com/FullMetalAlchemist/volume6.msnw?action=ShowPhoto&PhotoID=4246](http://groups.msn.com/FullMetalAlchemist/volume6.msnw?action=ShowPhoto&PhotoID=4246)

Edit 2: Also, Ed's state alchemist test is quite different on the manga.
[http://groups.msn.com/FullMetalAlchemist/volume6.msnw?action=ShowPhoto&PhotoID=4254](http://groups.msn.com/FullMetalAlchemist/volume6.msnw?action=ShowPhoto&PhotoID=4254)

LOL this is fun ^_^ !

Anyone interested in the manga, it's [_here_](http://groups.msn.com/FullMetalAlchemist/fullmetalalchemistmanga.msnw).

### Post by bored2k on 2005-07-28
**GunGRAVE: First impression :)**

The action scenes are pretty good (Grave's character and gun fighting style is _**very**_ similar to Christian Bale's in [Equilibrium](http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0238380/)). Similar to anime like Bebop, you get the feeling that a lot went on before the actual story "starts" wich is pretty cool and helps set the tone for this dark and gritty anime (the first 1-2 minutes reminded me a lot of vengeaful movies like The Crow or Punisher --not that they're any good, but this episode had a very strong Hollywood-esque feeling to it). Graphics are pretty good. Sound is just great. I had to watch it a second time with a higher volume because I wasn't getting all the bells & whistles of it. Music's very bass-oriented, rockish at times yet smooth. Sound effects are also top notch. GUNS (not guns, GUNS) sound as powerful as they should and the "Orcman"'s screams can get disturbing and graphic :D. Oh and Grave looks like a buffed up **D** from Vampire Hunter.

Great first impression.

[CENTER][IMG]http://img185.imageshack.us/img185/7424/screenshot05ki.jpg[/IMG][/CENTER] 

P.S. - Widescreen. Rocks :D.

---

### Post by bored2k on 2005-07-28
**GunGRAVE, take* two***

Ok now it's starting to look like a **really** good and complex series. If you watch the first episode you could get the idea that this is just simply another -- run and shoot no plot -- anime, well It's not. I won't even go to the minimum details because I don't want to spoil anything at all from it. This one was a lot more expressive and graphic, and the sound once again excels. I still think it's Very Hollywood oriented to a point where it's almost impossible to say it's pure casualty. Like I read yesterday on one of the numerous reviews I read, this is a **real** -if not _the_ real- "gangster" anime.

[CENTER][IMG]http://img192.imageshack.us/img192/5420/screenshot06gu.jpg[/IMG][/CENTER]

Edit : Episode three is just as great :D .

### Post by bored2k on 2005-07-28

 ** Dial-up readers, Run.. **

1. Bleach 42 ? It came out yesterday. It wasn't as "OMG!" as 41, but it was good, funny and and sexy (instead of the sexy cat.. well.. you'll see :-o). And btw, I'm loving the "Happy People" ending:D.

2. My GunGRAVE's subber is AonE. The whole 26 chapters are 4.3GB (.avi). I don't have complaints about the quality either, specially because it's a nice widescreen. It's not as good as A-Keep&Anbus' FMA one, but it's good. Here are some -unresized- pictures of it. 
Just a little note: I tried to get as steady and clear shots as I could but 1. They're _always_ moving and 2. The anime has a dark tone and in most of the scenes they certain color tends to take over (like, In a hospital it looks like a greenish radient, if there's a lot of violence it'll look red, yellow, etc). Font is most of the time reddish.

i recommend speed grapher... not just for the visual, but the story is very good.. people say that is one of the best newcomers this year ;)

### Post by bored2k on 2005-07-29
Fumoffu's ridiculously funny. I don't think I've seen anything like it before (although I've hardly seen comedy-centered anime -if ever-). Lol ridiculous. That's all I can think of right now. This one had me laughing for the whole 20 minutes, wich is very unlikely. Having watched the first episode of FMP and FMP:Fumoffu I can without a doubt say that Fomuffu's first impression is about 30 times better than the original. The first one even deals with the same issue (Sousuke being a complete ignorant ass, but they tell it in such a pale way it's not 1/8 as funny -IF funny at all-. I think GunGRAVE will have to sit down and wait til I finish this one (short one, should take me no more than 3 days) ;).

**How do they do it ?**

I don't know if I'm the only but, watching FMP:Fumoffu I get the feeling that I've watched a _really_ long episode. ### Post by bored2k on 2005-07-30
Finally I **finished** watching FMP:Fumoffu. I agree with sapo, this one's a must see. It's extremly goofy and funny. I think the real beauty of this anime is how the creator managed to _not_ make the comedic effect wear off overt time. Last chapter was pretty good also. Now I'm a little inclined over acquiring FMP and FMP:TSR, but that'll have to wait for GunGRAVE (although I will probably snatch some episodes in the mean time ;)). They might be only 15 episodes, but they really give you more than expected. In the first couple of episodes I honestly thought the ones named similar to  "06-07" were really 40 minute long episodes (I just loaded them up and pressed F for fullscreen w/o reading the length).

**[COLOR=DarkRed]Spoilers[/COLOR]: If you have not seen up to episode 11 of GunGRAVE, Do [COLOR=DarkRed]NOT[/COLOR] read !**

 Check your inbox.

By the way, If you must know foreheand, Rurouni Kenshin's main character is based on [Kawakami Gensai](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kawakami_Gensai), arguably the strongest and most frightful from the Hitokiri. 

If you must know the plot, it's about a Samurai that kicks ass, period. But I don't mean kick ass as in Samurai Champloo's where they hardly touch Mugen/Jin (until the end) and if they do 90% of the time is because they're drunk or high and so fast you don't even see it, I'm talking about _several_ episode long epic battles that don't grow old (unlike DBZ where Goku spends like a school year preparing the damn Spirit Bomb..).

In terms of Historical, it half feels like you're reading a History book. After you're done with it and start reading stuff about it, you'll read things like "X, Y and Z characters were actually real (etc)".

Oh yeah, last time I checked, the OVAs were amongst the most graphic ever.

### Post by NeoChaosX on 2005-08-02
Haha, can't believe I forgot to mention this series, and a favorite of mine: Midori no Hibi, aka Midori('s) Days.

[The background on this desktop here pretty much summarizes the series. Yeah, she's his right hand.](http://ubuntuforums.org/gallery/showimage.php?i=291&original=1&c=member&imageuser=5410)

It's about Seiji, a guy who's a complete badass at his school, yet can't get a girlfriend because of that aura. While he's lamenting his horrid love life, his right hand suddenly transforms in Midori, a girl's who's had a crush on him for a long time, but never admitted it to him. The series, despite being only 13 episodes long  (:mad: ), is definitely as sappy and silly as it's premise sounds, funny as hell yet still touching at times. The series' manga is also a good read, as it covers a lot more background and adventures than the short run of the anime. It's definately a keeper,at least for me.

---

Like everyone will say, the first two seasons own everything else; the third season is not that hot (after Shishio).

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-03
[QUOTE=sapo]so can i watch the 4 ovas then the movie then the tv series in this sequence?[/QUOTE]
 1. Reminiscence (Trust and Betrayal to some) - 4 OVAs
2. TV series - 95 episodes
3. Requiem for Patriots (Motion Picture) - 90 minute movie. 
4. Reflection (Seisouhen) - 2 OVAs

 Hmmm here's a tip I'll give you. Whenever Kenshin engages in a battle with a "respectable foe" or someone who poses a real threat, make sure you have at least 3 chapters on stock. If you watch the **epic** Saito battle a chapter every two hours you will really hurt yourself while waiting (don't worry, that's the in the 2nd season I believe ;)). There's no "30 second" battle like in Champloo on the series :-P. Trust me, I watched Kenshin one episode.. **A WEEK**. When it started, it wasn't really popular, but when it got kind of pop here, the TV channel restarted the whole thing after like 15 chapters.. more than once. Yes, it was a painful experience :( .

### Post by nickless on 2005-08-03
kenshin manga is great, the OAV the actually the best samurai action anime I have ever seen :D 
Still when I walk on a winter day outside, with snow everywhere and it's cold enough to see my breath I regret that I am not wearing a katana...
The series is kind of funny, but pretty boring, in my opinion :)

I have currently downloaded Steamboy, a shame that it didn't hit german movie theaters :( They spent 10 years of developing the movie...
And Trigun :D, one of the first animes I have seen ( the fist was agent aika), just got to watch it again, heh.
Ohh yeah I hope you all know agent aika? If you liked it, yeah its stupid, but that makes it funny, try colorful 
:D kind of FLCL meeting aika...

(otaku thread rocks!)

edit: One more thing: Whats your fav anime OST? :) Of course except CowboyBebop, because it is unbeatable... hrm or better lets say, your top 5 :

1.Cowboy Bebop (damn I want to see this on every list! )
2.FLCL (simply rocks )
3.Hellsing (cool-vampire-action-ambient) 
4.Chobits (nice, if your chilling in a "funny mood")
5.Trigun (nice chilling tracks and of course a kick-ass opening song)

(sadly kare kano didn't make it in my top 5, but I want to mention it, because it has some really good tracks for chilling at home. Ohh darn and the Samurai Champloo OST can't be forgotten totally,too )

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-03
[QUOTE=kumakun]On that note, MY gateway series was El Hazard. What series got all y'all into this..rather monitarily and temporally expensive hobby?[/QUOTE]
Ninja Scroll was a very good first impression for me a while back. I was so small I don't even remember but liking it (although I do remember some scenes because I knew some guys that were frustrated with it and kept renting it over and over).

Without counting Transformers or Voltron or whatever (I watched those because young -few years old- kids watch whatever cartoon's on at the moment), I had a gradual attraction to it. The **first** anime that I started following frantically was **Saint Seya**. I liked that one so much that I even refuse to download the new sequel that was made recently. Sometimes it's best to just leave some things to your memory, 'cause if you see/play them again, you might screw the idea you had about it being the best you had seen (this applies to everything: Hackers teh movie, I tried watching it the other day, almost got sick; Most of of the Playstation1 videogames -Super nintendo and older don't grow old ! Long live Zelda !-). A few months -or maybe a year- later, due to the fact that S-Seya woke kids up, they started broadcasting DB(then DBZ then the horrendous DB-GT). I liked the first two , but I wouldn't watch them for the world nowadays. My final and locking piece, the one that made me go bananas and wait for a weekly chapter like a wolf after its prey was **[COLOR=DarkRed]Rurouni Kenshin[/COLOR]**. I remember we liked that one so much we used to tape record it, so any given weekend me, my older brother and his best friend (wich now could arguably be mine too) would just watch several of our crappy Kenshin tapes and spend the whole week thinking about it.

Although I kind of forgot about anime for a long while simply because there wasn't anything that I could see after seeing Kenshin, a friend lended me his Evangelion DVDs and then out of curiosity and buzz I downloaded Cowboy Bebop without even seeing a trailer about it (just moved by a friend who liked it a lot). Even though I started collecting anime a few months ago, I'd still go bananas about anime everytime I saw one (no Toonami here :().

Heh, that ended up being longer than expected.. just skip to the part where it says Kenshin owns.. oh wait, you already read it !

---

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-05
[CENTER]**Bleach 43 **[/CENTER]

[CENTER]I don't know what you all think, but I think that was the most dramatic Bleach we've seen yet. [/CENTER]

It's nice to see after his last resort "power-up" Ishida is able to achieve captain level power (at least everything trembled when he got mad) and that he's able to get even better after learning his Quincy technique.

[CENTER][IMG]http://img22.imageshack.us/img22/2046/wow3fd.jpg[/IMG]
[/CENTER]

Also that **Kurotsuchi Mayuri **(12th Squad Captain) is so far -IMO- the most creative of the captains and arguably the whole cast. A scientist warrior that seems to think of everything as a scientifical study matter who has his own self-made "puppet" ? Ashisogi Jizou, his poisonous Soul Slayer ? His horn being a chain like weapon (we can see that on the next chapter's preview) ? Say what you want, that's creativity right there. I'm intrigued by this character.

[CENTER][IMG]http://img22.imageshack.us/img22/8182/i9vq.jpg[/IMG][/CENTER]

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-06
[QUOTE=Kyral]So now for Best Animes of All Time...

Kenshin (Collective)
Love Hina
Bleach
Gundam (Collective)

I feel safe with those 4 right now[/QUOTE]
I can not argue with the fact that Kenshin, Love Hina and Gundam (the fact that I don't like it doesn't mean that I don't appreaciate its greatness) should be on a top 10 -even top 5- list, but at the moment I don't think Bleach should still be elegible for that list. I mean come on ***K***, putting Bleach next to those three anime ? Saying Bleach is better than Fullmetal Alchemist and Cowboy Bebop (just to name a few) is a _huge_ statement. And even if it _was_ that good, we should at least wait for it to end. A lot of movies, tv series and yes, anime end up in a awfully shameful manner *(hint: GunGRAVE)*. 

Despite of me not believing it shouldn't still be on a top 5 ever list, I still think it rocks (behind FMA and Hina it's the best anime if seen in a while). The only scene I **ever** regret _hearing_  believe came on episode 40, wich is the one where Ichigo appears right when Byakuya was about to lay the smackdown (*lol*) on Kuchiki Rukia. The music was so horrendous I felt like my ears were going to pop out and explode. That *"If you wanna see some action..."* phrase sounded so corny, cheesy, lame and *GAAAAH* I can't hold it anymore ! Other than that, I love the music, specially when something is _about_ to happen (that weird, down tempo beat rocks).

### Post by YourSurrogateGod on 2005-08-07
[QUOTE=bored2k]I haven't seen Chobits. Is it good ? You seen Hina ? How do they square off ?[/QUOTE]
Chobits, from my point of view, was awesome. You can't compare it to Love Hina because they're completely different. In LH you have a loser who tries to get into a top-notch university while being a manager of an all-girls dorm. In Chobits, you have a loser (I guess they compare somewhat) who tries to get into a university and manages to find a persocon that was developed secretly and has a secret ability/mission. However, the found persocon has no memory of what happened before and therefore when the student boots it (I don't remember the guy's name) he finds out that he has to teach it everything from scratch (speech, basic things on taking care of yourself, etc.)

It's definitely worth a shot. So is Ai Yori Aoshi. None of those animes have any fighting in them, alot of comedy and suspense. Fun to watch.

BTW, I bought a dvd of ai yori aoshi enishi and the quality was abhorrent at best. Does anyone know of a place where I can get DVDs where the quality is decent?

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-07
[QUOTE=YourSurrogateGod]Yeh, dubbed suck for many other reasons. I tend to prefer subbed ones. I understand a few words, that and it just sounds better in japanese.[/QUOTE]
 Japanese totally sounds better. Most of the dubbed anime just sounds like some cheesy teen soap opera. What's bad about the dubs like the one on Love Hina is the fact that the english voice actors try to speak a 105% correct english, wich makes it sound even more awful (it's not a 'Learn English for dummies' anime, it's school life anime, speak like they should!). What's even worst is changing what the characters really said in order to cover it up and make the anime sound like it was aimed for a much younger audience. *ugh*

---

### Post by YourSurrogateGod on 2005-08-07
[QUOTE=bored2k]Japanese totally sounds better. Most of the dubbed anime just sounds like some cheesy teen soap opera. What's bad about the dubs like the one on Love Hina is the fact that the english voice actors try to speak a 105% correct english, wich makes it sound even more awful (it's not a 'Learn English for dummies' anime, it's school life anime, speak like they should!). What's even worst is changing what the characters really said in order to cover it up and make the anime sound like it was aimed for a much younger audience. *ugh*[/QUOTE]
I've noticed that not just dubs can be crappy, but also subs. After I downloaded Chobits, I bought the DVD set. My first thought was "Great! I have a legit copy of my favorite anime! Now I can throw out my burned CDs." When I watched the first episode I began kicking myself for throwing out those CDs, the translation in the subs was such a pain to read to :-x . I think I should simply download anime that I like, save it and then buy a legit copy so as not to screw the producer of the stuff. 

Don't get me even started on the Ai Yori Aoshi subs... which reminds me, I know I asked this question before, but where can I buy good quality DVDs that are legit, that have very well translated sub-titles? I'd like to have a library of that stuff one day...

---

### Post by kumakun on 2005-08-07
[QUOTE=YourSurrogateGod]I've noticed that not just dubs can be crappy, but also subs. After I downloaded Chobits, I bought the DVD set. My first thought was "Great! I have a legit copy of my favorite anime! Now I can throw out my burned CDs." When I watched the first episode I began kicking myself for throwing out those CDs, the translation in the subs was such a pain to read to :-x . I think I should simply download anime that I like, save it and then buy a legit copy so as not to screw the producer of the stuff. 

Don't get me even started on the Ai Yori Aoshi subs... which reminds me, I know I asked this question before, but where can I buy good quality DVDs that are legit, that have very well translated sub-titles? I'd like to have a library of that stuff one day...[/QUOTE]

Chances are if you find what you think are well translated subtitles, what you'll actually find are poor attempts at fan subs. The quality of subtitling has gone up quite a bit. In the old days they ballparked the meaning and ran with it. The reality is that anything that has different subtitles from the ones you'd find in a boxed DVD at say, media play, won't be legit. However, Manga tend to be more true to the original language. Take that scene from Love Hina that Bored refrenced. It's translated correctly there.

Personally I like subs and dubs. There are things I can't watched Subtitled, like Trigun. "Rabu and Pisu!" No way. There are some things I can't watch dubbed, too, for example Ai Yori Aoshi anytime after Tina is introduced. Love Hina's Kitsune does the same thing to me, but it's not as glaring as Tina's voice. Wendee Lee will forever sound like Wendee Lee.

/K

Now that were having a **sane and civilized** talk about fansubs, I think you all would be interested in reading (if you already have not) these two ANN editorials about Fansubs and BitTorrent back when BT was basically in diapers (2003).
[B][CENTER][Unethical Fansubbers (Jun 8 2003)](http://animenewsnetwork.com/editorial.php?id=43) 
[Unethical Fansubbers - Followup (Jun 19 2003)](http://animenewsnetwork.com/editorial.php?id=44) [/CENTER][/B]

[CENTER][B]
[BitTorrent - The future of fansubs? (Jun 8 2003)[/B]](http://animenewsnetwork.com/editorial.php?id=39) [/CENTER]
I couldn't think of a better way to distribute fansubs than BitTorrent. Apparently, everyone felt the same way back in 2003, they just didn't know how _big_ would fansub distribution get :roll: .

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-07
[QUOTE=kumakun]Additionally, with dubbing, keep in mind that they have to fit the spoken lines to the mouth movements. It gets increasingly difficult where animated mouth movements become more and more refined and less like an open and closed mouth in one position over and over.
/K[/QUOTE]
I would understand that the english audio is not in synch with the mouth movements. After all, they are two different (way different) languages. But in the Love Hina example I mentioned, its just horrendous. I don't know if you've seen the anime, but let me verbose a bit on my example:
[INDENT]A boy walks in his grand mother's house. It has been turned into a girls dorm (he does not know this). Walking around he sees a newly construced hot spring and "hey, wth", jumps in. Shortly after, this girl (that can't see jack without glasses) enters the hot spring, and thinking that the person inside of the water is one of her friends comes _very_ close to "her" and says "Look my breasts are getting bigger" while letting "her" touch them.[/INDENT]
Now if you have never seen it in the original japanese voice, you would get "Come here, hug me". What would you understand? "Why the hell would this chick tell the other to hug her in a nice and sexy voice, while they're both basically naked ?". You would simply think you are watching a lesbian based anime. THat's my beef with dubs.

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-07
[QUOTE=kumakun]Meh. Whatever, I liked it. I like dubs most of the time, unless it's truely truely horrific (Ai Yori's Tina). It's the lazy factor. I throw in a DVD when I'm on the computer, so I don't typically have the inclination to be reading subtitles.

/K[/QUOTE]
 Well yeah, reading while following the action is what we all try to run from, but there are some anime (ok, most of them) that I would only like in their original language. It's actually not only anime, but movies and tv series. Being from a spanish speaking country, I have learned one thing: watch movies, tv, anime, or whatever in their _original_ language. Portuguese, french, japanese, chinese, you name it, its just never as good. Good examples are [Cidade de Deus](http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0317248/) , [Maria Llena de Gracia](http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0390221/), **any** chinese movie (I could watch old kung fu flicks in english but that's because they're HILARIOUS and even more entertaining than the action itself ;)), **any** Jim Carrey movie _not_ in english (In spanish it's just.. not right). For the spanish dubs for Shrek and The Incredibles they had to basically come up with a new set of jokes that went with the scene, because actors like Eddie Murphy aren't easily dubbed.

I'm not bashing or anything, I respect everyone's opinion, but what I'm trying to show is why I prefer original languages over 90% of what I see (spanish Kenshin was ok; English Kenshin is not terrible although Kenshin loses it's originally acted female voice --the character Kenshin was based on had this girly traits).

In short,
Rock _on :D


Off topic - Reading some wikis about an anime led me to read about ecchi wich led me to read about H-Games wich led me to led about hentai wich finally led me to read about Japanese pornography and its acceptance/rejection in the culture. Good reading :) .

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-08
> The oldest animated film created in Japan and screened has been found in Kyoto. Up until now the oldest Japanese animation was believed to be Shimokawa Hekoten's "Imokawa Mukozo the Doorman," from 1917, but this newfound animation, on 35mm film, could be as much as ten years older.

Matsumoto Natsuki, a part time lecturer at the Osaka-Tokyo University of Arts and Music found the 50-frame film in an old family projector in Kyoto amongst a collection of foreign animation. It was hand-drawn in two colors, red and black, directly onto the celluloid. The creator is unknown.

In the first decade of the 1900s there were very few cinemas in Japan and only the wealthy owned projectors.

The animation depicts a young boy wearing a sailor suit writing "katsudoushashin" (movie) on a blackboard, turning around to face the audience and saluting. At 16 frames per second, the animation only lasts 3 seconds.

**posted on 2005-08-07 01:03:07**

I'm amazed there was no Gundam back then :roll: . I thought they were making Gundam before Columbus discovered America ..

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-08
ANN has a nice subjective and quite funny article on DearS:
> [IMG]http://animenewsnetwork.com/images/releases/4360.jpg[/IMG] 
Every bone in my body is telling me that I should hate DearS. It has a paper-thin plot, stereotypical characters, a disturbing amount of male chauvinism, and the depth of a cookie sheet. But you know what, Ren is so goshdarned cute I can’t hold a grudge against the show. Adapted from the manga by Peach Pit, the artwork is best described as “docile.” All the characters have big doe eyes, flowing hair, and pretty faces that bubble with life—that is, everyone except the main character and his neighbor. Takuya finds a naked girl by the side of the road and finds out that she’s a DearS, a race of aliens that crash-landed on Earth a year earlier. Professing to be his slave for eternity, she follows him home and manages to wriggle her way into his life. For those who have seen Chobits, the resemblance will be immediately obvious, but the parallels eventually fade. Unfortunately, those are replaced with other clichés, like the childhood-friend neighbor who has a crush on the main guy, the promiscuous teacher who prances around the classroom in her skivvies, and cooking competitions. Ultimately, what’s the most discomforting about this show is the odd twist in fanboy pandering. All the DearS women are perfect models of how women should be, yet they profess to be nothing more than slaves to their masters. While I’m sure that’s a perfectly welcome thought to the target audience, it’s a little disgusting. Even with all the downsides though, DearS still remains a relatively entertaining show. It doesn’t do anything more than entertain, but with the cute character designs and the chipper characters, it’s worth a light-hearted rental.

Related Products: For another shounen romance series involving unhuman sweethearts with big gushy eyes serving men, check out Chobits or Mahoromatic.
Tastes Like: Melon bread. Actually, I have no idea what it tastes like, but they sure do make it look cute in the series.

Man, dont say that i didnt adviced you... but please, watch Elfen Lied, its just 12 episodes, then come back and give us a feedback...  :roll:

Its stupid, its for kids but have violence and hentai, the story is stupid, the chars are stupid, the end is stupid.. this anime IS stupid.

before i ve downladed, my friend (girl) said, "sapo, dont do it, i hate Elfen Lied, please dont download it!", i said "its just 12 eps, i ll give it a try". The first episode r0x, but the rest sux!

When i finished watching i talked to her and said "I ll listen to your advice next time, Elfen Lied Really sux.".


About GITS, take a look:
[http://forum.anime-reactor.net/viewtopic.php?t=6219](http://forum.anime-reactor.net/viewtopic.php?t=6219)

It just OWNED the 2003 awards

hehehe ok ok ill say what i think about those animes...


**Best Anime:**

FMA - Deserve everything that won.. it r0x!

**Best Newcomer:**

**School Rumble - Gundam SEED DESTINY / Mai-HiME** - Didnt watch mai-Hime (i ll put it on the queue) but school Rumble and GSD r0x and deserve the price too!

**Best Animation 2004:**

Ghost in the Shell: INNOCENCE  - Perfect, the animation REALLY is awesome!

Best Story 2004:

**Full Metal Alchemist - Monster** - The 2 animes have that freaking awesome story.. deserved too!

**Best OP/ED-Combination 2004:**

Elfen Lied - Kannazuki no Miko  - Didnt watch the second one, but Elfen Lied sux!, but there are still kids out there to vote on a freaking stupid story like that...

**Best OST:**

MADLAX - Thats ok, not my music style but the ost is good, kinda different from the rest of the animes.. ;)

**Best Male Character 2004:**

Edward Elric -Madarame Harunobu - Didnt watch genshiken, but Ed rlz ;)

**Best Female Character 2004:**

Lucy/Nyuu(Elfen Lied) - Kasukabe Saki(Genshiken) - Lol.. how can they name "best" female character to a double personality girl, how in one side is like a little kid that just says "nyu, nyu, nyu" and in the other just kill everything that cross her path? is so damn stupid!

**Best Villain 2004**

Johann Liebert - (Monster) - Johann is the best villain.. dont dare to complain :D


**Best Character Development 2004**

Planetes - Didnt watch.


**Best Action / Martial Arts 2004**

Samurai Champloo - Tenjou Tenge - I m watching tenjou tenge right now, speaking in "fights" and martial arts it is better than samurai champloo, but samurai champloo kicks asses in the overall anime.


**Best Adventure / Fantasy 2004**

Full Metal Alchemist - Dont need to explain :D


**Best Drama 2004**

Elfen Lied - Koi Kaze - grrrr Elfen Lied again.. to hell with this stupid anime! its the stupidiest "drama" i ve ever seen! And i Didnt watch Koi Kaze.. so i dont know about it.. but im sure its better than elfen lied  :roll: 


**Best Romance 2004**

Koi Kaze - Dunno about it...


**Best Comedy 2004**

Genshiken - Its in my queue.


**Best Fanservice / Ecchi 2004**

Elfen Lied - Tenjou Tenge - WHY WHY ELFEN LIED AGAIN?

**Best Sci-Fi / Mecha 2004**

Ghost in the Shell - S.A.C. 2nd GIG - Planetes - this planetes must be good to be side by side with Gits in the award... gits ownz!

**Best Mystery / Thriller / Horror 2004**

Elfen Lied - Monster - man.. how they dare to compare monster with this shit?


**Best Quick Watch 2004**

Tokyo Godfathers - Have to watch it too!

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-10
**1. **[CENTER]**Bleach anime 43 = Bleach manga 123**[/CENTER]

I think the manga corresponding to the 43rd is an interesting one and should be read by anyone interested in the anime. The manga's even more disturbing (look at the link below) :-/

[CENTER][[IMG]http://img362.imageshack.us/img362/4618/mangarainbleachch123146ud.th.png[/IMG]](http://img362.imageshack.us/my.php?image=mangarainbleachch123146ud.png) (...) [[IMG]http://img134.imageshack.us/img134/7426/mangarainbleachch123195uu.th.png[/IMG]](http://img134.imageshack.us/my.php?image=mangarainbleachch123195uu.png)[/CENTER]

**2.**
Bleach manga gets released once a week right ? If so, could the anime catch up with it ?

The reason I'm asking this is because so far there are 189 manga episodes and the anime (chapter 43) ends on chapter 123 of the manga and that episode alone was about 4 grouped manga chapters. At this rate ( Weekly anime=4 manga chapters; Weekly manga ), the anime would have to rush and come up *first* with the story (just like happened with Kenshin, right before its quality went down..).

 So you've read it ? Ok some questions roll in my head. 
- After the story on the anime ends, how much further does the manga continue ?
- Just how close (or not) does the anime follow the manga ?

---

### Post by kumakun on 2005-08-13
[QUOTE=bored2k]So you've read it ? Ok some questions roll in my head. 
- After the story on the anime ends, how much further does the manga continue ?
- Just how close (or not) does the anime follow the manga ?
- Is there anything _drastically_ different worth mentioning ?

Not that I won't read it (I intend to), but I'm just curious.[/QUOTE]

Read it, padawan? I own all 14 lucious volumes. The anime ends somewhat differently than the manga does, however, from the point you see at the end of Love Hina Again, it continues with a jump of...4 years. The anime, for the most part, stays faithful to the manga. It starts to split off at LH:A, but everything you see in the anime has an incarnation in the manga. And yes, there IS drastically different stuff, but I can't mention it, cause it's spoiler. However, the Manga is all the better for it.

Enjoy! Let me know what you think.

In the meantime, those of you looking for something to watch, I just finished Chrono Crusade the other day. Bloody hell I bawled like a school girl with a skinnedknee. It was great.

/K

### Post by kumakun on 2005-08-13
[QUOTE=bored2k]
Oh I'm looking for something *me me!*. Can you tell us more about Chrono ? I've read Animenewsnetwork's and AniDB's take on it, but I'm still a little lost on what it's about and what does the anime focus on (Action, fantasy, drama, etc).[/QUOTE]

Okay, so it's 1928, and in new york the roaring 20's are in full swing. The level of decadence and prosperity in the country gives surge to a new pass time of the wealthy: Devil Summoning.

Enter the Magdalene Order. These men and women are spiritual gunslingers, keeping humanity safe from themselves. Two of the members of the Magdalene Order are a young girl of 16 named Rosette Christopher and her partner, a Devil named Chrono. 

There is a LOT to the story. Back story about Chrono and the villian of the tale, Aion. What it all boils down to is trying to fathom God's Plan and trying to divert it. And ultimately not doing well at that last part. 

This series will play on exisiting history. For example, it's pre world war 2. There are several references to the Fatima Prophecies, 2 of which have come to pass. It's not something to expect a happy ending from. After all, look at the state of the world today.

Try it on, you'll love it. I went in expecting "Cool! Nun's with guns!" And I got drawn into an incredible story with a very touching ending.

/K

### Post by Kyral on 2005-08-14
And Gundam SEED Destiny 43 is out now. And of course I have a quick review.

A lot happens in this episode, Athrun returns to the Justice and beats down Shinn, Kira and Rey go after each other, Juna dies (FINALLY!!), Mwu starts to recover his memories, and the fake Lacus is finally exposed, in grand fashion. ### Post by Kyral on 2005-08-15
Okay, finished the Love Hina Manga and I must say it is better than the anime, what with Volume 13 - 14 on there. Its nice to see what happens at the HinataSou, and to see Kei and Naru get married. Next I start with Ah! My Goddess and Rurouni Kenshin

### Post by sapo on 2005-08-17
i ve found this:
[http://www.answers.com/topic/himura-kenshin](http://www.answers.com/topic/himura-kenshin)

very good stuff :D

> 
The following is a short list of his techniques:

**Nidan Batt&#333;jutsu:**

**Hi Ryu Sen**: an unusual and very risky technique. Considered a battoujutsu technique, Kenshin holds the sheath with the sword still inside, handle facing his enemy, and thrusts his arm outward, flicking the tsuba of the sword with his thumb at the same time. The sword is flung out of the sheath at a high speed and strikes the opponent.

**Ka Ryu Sen**: a gust of wind caused by Kenshin's sakabat&#333;, he uses this to stun or impede opponents at a distance.

**Ryu Tsui Sen**: Kenshin jumps and slashes down using his body weight to increase the blow's strength.

**Ryu Kan Sen/Ryu Kan Zen Tsumuji**: a circular, outwards slash that goes up. The tsumuji is the consecutive attack in which Kenshin spins to deliver another slash.

**S&#333;yumi**: Kenshin rarely uses this attack, it involves him holding his sword pointing at his enemy and spinning in a series of forward somersaults.

**D&#333; Ryu Sen/Terrestrial Dragon Ligh**t: This technique involves a strike at the soil which projects a hail of rocks or fragments at the enemy.

**Kuzu Ryu Zen/Multi-headed Dragon Ligh**t: Kenshin learned this attack when he headed to Kyoto to complete his training with Hiko Seijur&#333;. This technique attacks all nine possible directions in lightning-fast succession. The attack is considered non-escapable or blockable in the series with two exceptions: the Amakakeru Ryu No Hirameki and the Shukushi.

**Amakakeru Ryuu no Hirameki**: this is Hiten Mitsurugi-Ry&#363;'s &#333;gi (ultimate technique, the attack one must master to be called Shishou and to become the next Seijuro Hiko). This attack is a modification of the battou structure of attack. Usually the batt&#333; attack involves doing three footsteps and then drawing the sword. The ougi has the user do two and a half footsteps and draw the sword at the same time as one does the third footstep. This results in a unusually fast slash that generates a displacement of air in the sword's trail. The vacuum prevents the adversary from moving or blocking. Seijuro Hiko calls this move, "the Dragon's claws from which one can't escape".

**Kenshin Ryu Spinning Circular Attack**: in Samurai X: The Motion Picture (Requiem for the Ishin Patriots), Kenshin faces against an old enemy who witnessed his best friend's death at Kenshin's hands. He therefore learned all of Kenshin moves and countered them with his sword and sheath attack style. As his last resort, Kenshin jumped up and fooled his opponent into believing that he was going to use the Ryu Tsui Zen. Kenshin then spun around and delivered an airborne, spinning attack that was very similar to the Amakakeru Ryu No Hirameki. Sagara Sanosuke named this attack.

---

**Here is a full list of all Hiten Mitsurugi-Ry&#363; techniques, both Kenshin's and his master's:**

**Hiten Mitsurugi-Ry&#363;: Doryusen (Ground Dragon Slash)** = Giant blow against the ground, causing massive airwaves/rocks to strike enemies.

**Hiten Mitsurugi-Ry&#363;: Ry&#363;tsuisen (Dragon Hammer Slash)** = Jumps up in the air really high and then strikes down hard from above.

**Hiten Mitsurugi-Ry&#363;: Ry&#363; Kan Sen (Spiral Dragon Slash)** = Lunges toward enemy using a very powerful twist of the body doing a sideways slash.

**Hiten Mitsurugi-Ry&#363;: Ry&#363;s&#333;sen (Dragon's Nest Slash)** = Multiple Slashes against enemy.

**Hiten Mitsurugi-Ry&#363;: Ry&#363;kansen Tsumuji (Rising Wind Dragon Slash Hair Spin)** = Tsumuji (gyrate) involves higher speed than Ryukansen. Basically same attack but more powerful.

**Hiten Mitsurugi-Ry&#363;: Ry&#363;sh&#333;sen (Dragon Rising Flash)** = Rises up from ground, using all force in an uppercut, hitting vital points.

**Hiten Mitsurugi-Ry&#363;: Ryutsuisen Zan** = Same as Ryutsuisen except sword is pointed down to pierce.

**Hiten Mitsurugi-Ry&#363;: Hiryusen (Flying Dragon Slash)** = Performed by left hand only, attack uses centrifugal force to create a powerful blow with the hilt in a person's weakness, like in between the eyes.

**Hiten Mitsurugi-Ry&#363;: Ryukansen Arashi (Wind Dragon Rising Slash Storm)** = More powerful version of Tsumuji + more damaging.

**Batt&#333;-jutsu Techniques**

**Batt&#333;-jutsu (Art of Drawing the Blade)** = Stance with right foot placed in front of left, pressing the blade of the sheathed sword against the curve of the sheath, which increases draw speed threefold. The blade is then drawn by the entire body's motion, slashing with the "God-speed of Hiten Mitsurugi-Ry&#363; ". It is an all-or-nothing attack where missing the target will result in the user being completely unguarded. Kenshin's hitokiri name, "Batt&#333;sai", means "One who has mastered everything of batt&#333;-jutsu"

**Hiten Mitsurugi-Ry&#363;: Soryusen (Paired Dragon Flash)** = Paired attack with sword and sheath. If sword is blocked, sheath comes in and hits enemy.

**Defensive Techniques**

**Zantetsu = Iron cutting technique**. Can use it anywhere excempt underwater.

**Shihara Dori** = Defensive technique in which user grabs enemies sword.

**Ryumeisan** = User quickly sheathes sword, causing a sound that distorts the hearing of those with enhanced senses.

**Modoshi Giri (Reversing Cut)** = User can cut something in half and then rejoin it together and make it seem as if there was no cut whatsoever.

**Special Techniques**

**Hiten Mitsurugi-Ry&#363;: Ry&#363;tsuish&#333;sen (Dragon Hammer Rising Slash)** = Combo of Ry&#363;tsuisen and Ry&#363;sh&#333;sen. Performed when opponent is coming down from above, Ry&#363;tsuisen first, then Ry&#363;sh&#333;sen, causing double damage.

**Hiten Mitsurugi-Ry&#363;: Kogarashi Tsumuji Arashi** (no translation) = First he starts off turning around and doing one hit, then jumps and hits again, then uses the turning momentum to go into a spin and slicing again. Spin goes vertically, not horizontally. Now for the two most powerful techniques that Kenshin learns from his master Hiko Seijuuro in the series.

**Hiten Mitsurugi-Ry&#363;: Kuzuryusen (Nine Headed Dragon Flash)** = Attack learned before the most powerful succesion technique. Strikes with the sword nine times on the 9 vital points, head, right leg, left leg, right arm, left arm, between the legs, the chest, right shoulder, and left shoulder, making this move virtually impossible to block.

**Succession Technique**

**Hiten Mitsurugi-Ry&#363;: Ougi Ama Kakeru Ryu No Hirameki(Heaven's Soaring Dragon Slash)** = THE SUCCESSION TECHNIQUE. Normally when using the Battojutsu, the right leg is placed in front of the left leg so as not to cut one's leg off when drawing your sword from the left, but with this one put his or her left leg in front giving your attack the most power it can get, and he or she must twist his or her body at the right time so as not to cut his or her leg off. In order to do this he must have a strong will to live. This move is actually a combination of two, because if this move is executed properly, if the first hit is blocked, then the person spins around, and slices again. It works because apparently after the first strike, the sword somehow makes a kindof vortex, sucking in the enemy, allowing the person to be close enough to be sliced again, kind of like a vacuum.

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-18
[QUOTE=psychicdragon]I like Kim a lot too and I'm way better with him in KoF. Terry is just a really cool character. He's really strong in the Fatal Fury games. I like most of the characters from the Fatal Fury series.

I'm not such a fan of Tekken but I haven't played Tekken 4 or 5 much. Tekken 5 is still $1 at the arcade. When they drop it to .50 I'll give some more play time. 

For me the best fighters are:
Real Bout Fatal Fury 2
Fatal Fury: MotW[/QUOTE]
 Garou: MotW is an awsome game. Although, I don't like Fatal Fury games very much, I enjoy the King  of Fighters saga. They're just massive, in your face action that's basically an upgrade on RB:FF (although Spek SNK took FF in a different direction with Garou:MotW). In MotW my favorite characters would probably be Terry Bogard, Rock Howard,  Kim Jae Hoon and Kim Dong Hwan. But overall, King of Fightes is the best and biggest monster Spek-SNK ever created.

Tekken? I won't even stress on Tekken. Best fighter period. Tekken 3 is IMO the best fighter ever period. Tekken 4 was not good - it was slow and non inspiring. Tekken Tag was definitely OK because it was Tekken 3 redux, and although Tekken 5 is REALLY an upgrade over all the Tekken flicks, it didn't "wow" me as much as Tekken 3 did. The hours I've spent sharping my tekken skills are countless. Get me a controller and Tekken .. that's all I need

---

### Post by psychicdragon on 2005-08-18
I use to play a loads of Tekken 3 for the Playstation back in highschool. Tekken Tag kinda sucked and I stopped with the series there I guess. I've always been more of a fan of 2d fighters anyway.

The early Fatal Fury games were garbage but Real Bout and Mark of the Wolves are some of the finest old school 2d fighting anywhere.

I'm sick of the KoF series. It hasn't looked good in years and there always seem to be balance issues. The games in the NEST's saga turned me off a lot. I also don't like the change from the vibrant colors of the earlier games to the new darker looking sprites. 97 and 98 looked so incredibly good. 2002 looks like crap. After the one year hiatus they recently took, I was hoping to see some new, better animated sprites. Looks like that's never going to happen though.
After saying all that I must admit I am still known to play KoF 2002 when there's some action at the machine.
