### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-18
[QUOTE=psychicdragon]I use to play a loads of Tekken 3 for the Playstation back in highschool. Tekken Tag kinda sucked and I stopped with the series there I guess. I've always been more of a fan of 2d fighters anyway.[/QUOTE]
A lot of fans were turned off by TTT and never came back to Tekken. That happened right when Sould Edge went through puberty and became the latest Namco heavy-hitter Soul Calibur. I guess the Tekken's days of glory ended abruptly after TTT (although I like it, I'd rather play Tekken 3). 

Oh and for the record, they completely f___d up Jin Kazama in Tekken 4 ! From Mishima Style of Karate to that crap monks taught him (best story in a fighter period), I found myself shocked when I grabbed Tekken 4 and found out my #2 fighter was DOA (dead on arrival, not Tecmos' crappy tag fighter :P). They tried to fix there enormous mistake by including Kazuya Mishima and Jun Kazama in Tekken, but they're not half as good as Jin was (although Kazuya sucked, Jun was just a 1/4 strip of Jin's moves and still rocked).

And yeah, 2D fighters rock. The only two 3D fighters that made my head shake like a bubble-head for hours was Tekken and Soul_Calibur_2 (I don't play it anymore). I tried giving Virtua Fighter 4 a shot but It just didn't felt right enough.

[QUOTE=psychicdragon]The early Fatal Fury games were garbage but Real Bout and Mark of the Wolves are some of the finest old school 2d fighting anywhere.[/QUOTE]That's right. I have to admit I admire Spek-SNK's will to try out different fighters until they got their right. Kizuna Encounter ? Sengoku ? Art of Fighting (you exclude Ryo Sakazaki and Robert Garcia --two of KOF's finest- and you have nothing but an expensive SNK cartridge) ? Fatal Fury 1,2 (I played those a lot on SNES) ? Samurai Showdown ? .. no wait, SS was a good one. Props to Spek_SNK for their wild creativity (they weren't simple skin changed games like companies do nowadays *cough*Capcom*cough*).

[QUOTE=psychicdragon]I'm sick of the KoF series. It hasn't looked good in years and there always seem to be balance issues. The games in the NEST's saga turned me off a lot. I also don't like the change from the vibrant colors of the earlier games to the new darker looking sprites. 97 and 98 looked so incredibly good. 2002 looks like crap. After the one year hiatus they recently took, I was hoping to see some new, better animated sprites. Looks like that's never going to happen though.
After saying all that I must admit I am still known to play KoF 2002 when there's some action at the machine.[/QUOTE]I didn't say the latest KoF were any the best or anything, I'm just saying overall the series rocks (thanks to NeoRageX for letting me play all of them at will -when I did play- :D). Yeah, I see what you mean, the series started to get grayish after when they brought **K'** and the gazillion Kyo in KoF 99'. Rugal owned. Kryzalid does not. And yes, the balance on the fighters is awful. Kyo, Maxima, Clark et al are too powerful and yet they toned down juggernauts like Ryo and Robert in the past few years. But still, I kinda like how all they managed to mesh everyone in one single game. And hey, if you screaming about balance issues, get any Marvel vs Capcom *lol*. Get Jill Valentine against The Hulk, Juggernaut or Zangief.. ouch. Character butchery ? Pick anyone between Ryu, Akuma or Ken in MvsC 1.. oh wait, they are just one character !

Notice how I didn't even mention KOF 3D ..


Edit: A Terry Bogard avatar is all it took for me to turn the Otaku thread into a videogame one lol.

This poll was made on a very popular anime *database*, and I just thought I'd post it here (all of you have probably seen it already).
 Here I can see a few interesting things:
[list=1]

[*]There are **more** anime lovers using Linux than those on Mac! Maybe Mac computers come prebundled with Pixar/Disney hipnotizing software :D.

[/list]

Hey,

I watched Vampire Hunter D: Bloodlust last night. I liked the first one better.

The animation was amazing. Obviously much better than the original  :grin: but it lacked some of its precursors style. I liked the purple color of D's cloak for instance. The wolfman looked really awesome. All the female characters looked bad.

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-20
[QUOTE=sapo]Why not download the mkv version?  :|[/QUOTE]
 It rocks. Tri audio: professional japanese, original japanese and english (creator and fiancée's voice). Supposedly all three of them are quite different and have different lines from each other wich means you'll watch the anime at least thrice if you like it :D.

### Post by YourSurrogateGod on 2005-08-20
[QUOTE=bored2k]It rocks. Tri audio: professional japanese, original japanese and english (creator and fiancée's voice). Supposedly all three of them are quite different and have different lines from each other wich means you'll watch the anime at least thrice if you like it :D.[/QUOTE]
See, the thing that's so great about this anime is not the fact that it's great in it of it self. It's a good anime and a number of high-profile companies could have produced this stuff. But what *really* stands out was the fact that it was a guy and a girl (his wife/fiance) and a Mac (this is one of those few instances that I wont' bash macs.) That man created this masterpiece (notice how I use "masterpiece'), not only is it awesome because it was one guy who did it, but because the anime rivals everything else that I've ever seen.

The guy should get a nobel prize in anime.

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-21
[B]Voices of a Distant Star
[/B]It's definitely a very sad story  :-& . It's kind of the *worst* case scenario and then some **more**. You can't really talk about it without spoiling anything (plus being short in length) so I'll just say this is an anime that deserves to be seen more than once. Although the story's not "Evangelion" complicated, I don't think you'd get the most out of it by just watching it one time. I just finished watching it for the first time and I'm already thinking about watching it again with the _other_ japanese track (although I'm not sure I'd watch this one in its third language - English) in a random, non "scripted" day I feel calm and perceptive enough to do so.

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-21
*D'autre part*, today I finished **FLCL**, wich means I was on the hunt for new anime again. A few minutes ago I got **quite** bubbly because I could not seem to be able to get a hold of Chrono Crusade. I tried every url I could find and they were all pretty much hopeless (I'm amazed such an anime has not been re-seeded !). There's a -supposedly- working and active KAA (a release group) version around, but it's still in development [Two batches have been released: 01-08 and 09-12 (the 2nd one came roughly one month after the first)] so I won't get that one because I don't feel like waiting 1-2 months waiting for an anime to unfold after I've seen half of it !

So what did I decide to pick up ? [COLOR=Navy]Last Exile[/COLOR]. I've read nothing but greatness from it and someone on #ubuntuforums told me it rocked. It looks like it has a RPG-esque feeling to it so I'm giving that one a shot (Open ended , fantastic -but not enough to make me sick- and creative "RPG" style is what made me love FMA so much). By the screenshots alone it looks mindblowing. Being a Top-10 anime on AniDB doesn't hurt either ;).

[ANN Info](http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=2294) 
[ANN DVD 1 Review](http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/reviews/display.php?id=587)

> **nickless]

Problem #1: You didn't see enough of it to really know what the anime is about. It's not a looser wants girlfriend flick. It's the life of a goofy boy managing a girls dormitory AND the life of his wacky, unique and interesting customers. One boy + Girls dorm = Awkward situations. It's really good. I could understand someone not liking it (true, free opinion, where in a linux forum after all), but you were probably biased and didn't understand it  said:**
> 
You know in every "fictional" story you read or see, there is some "supernatural" thing or, well "fictional" thing, that the watcher has to accept as realistic in this type of setting. Still, I respect your opinion ;). Hina rocks hard.

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-21
A couple of small reviews on what I just saw:

[INDENT]**[FONT=Comic Sans MS]FLCL 02[/FONT]**[/INDENT]
At first I thought "hmm, maybe the director's stash of *candy* ran out on this one" until I saw the battle between TV-robot-guy and the Bigger-scrap-metal. What can I say, these two eps have been great. The two FLCL eps could have been the best things I watched today, until...

[INDENT]**[FONT=Century Gothic]Last Exile 01[/FONT]**[/INDENT]
I wouldn't call this one a great first impression, I'd call it Love at First Sight. Just like the ANN review said, you could just forget about the subs and sit down and watch this visual achievement. This episode alone is in a league of its down (and its just supposedly a "teaser" for what's coming). The sound effects (WOW). The MUSIC --> it's just TOO much. An anime TV series is not supposed to sound this good ! I mean, if someone told me "oh, that's an ultra big budget OVA" I would completely believe it. The DRAMA of war... it's just too much. Myriads of battleships (airborne battleships) mawling each other and a mere soldier *cough*pawn*cough* praying he's not next while his comrades fall next to him, man that was awsome. Imma stop talking before I jump from my balcony while trying to fly.

---

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-22
[CENTER]Animenewsnetwork.com previews **Bleach**
[http://animenewsnetwork.com/reviews/display.php?id=819](http://animenewsnetwork.com/reviews/display.php?id=819)[/CENTER]
> But wait, isn't there plenty of fight-centric adventure anime already? Yes, but Bleach sets itself apart with a cast of characters that couldn't exist anywhere else. Imagine the usual stereotypes, but skew them with odd traits, and give their personalities an extra edge of attitude: that's what makes these characters special.

(...)

Who can forget the funny logos on Ichigo's t-shirts, Rukia's single bang hanging over her face, Ishida's rectangular glasses, Orihime's hair clips, Chad's scraggly features, Urahara's green-and-white striped hat, Renji's visor that looks like a sleep mask... well, this could go on for a while.

(...)

So is it really just Naruto with dead people? Or is it an epic saga in its own right? The unique characters and look of Bleach guarantee that it will, at the very least, rise above the muddle of interchangeable shounen series and march on with its own unique vision of the afterlife.

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-23
**Love Hina [COLOR=DarkRed]Christmas[/COLOR] Special**

[FONT=Trebuchet MS][SIZE=3]Absolutely fantastic[/SIZE][/FONT]. I really liked the fact that this 45 minutes special was not simply a longer episode with the usual "goofy guy and the girl's occurrences" nor a 45 minute ecchi fest. Instead, it delivers as a very sweet stand alone romantic special with no need for ecchi to be any good. As usual, the heart of this anime --its characters-- gets the story going gracefully.

[CENTER][IMG]http://img379.imageshack.us/img379/2087/screenshot04rc.jpg[/IMG] [/CENTER]

**Animation: 10/10**
Can't you see how good it looks?!
**Sound: 8.5/10**
Not really bad, but nothing really stands out.
**Story: 9/10**
The plot thickens and the scenes get more powerful one after the other.
**Enjoyment: 10/10**
I had a _really_ good time watching these 45 minutes.
**Value: 9.5/10**
Anyone who watched the TV episodes needs to see this.
**Characters: 12/10 ^__^**
The eclectic cast is tighter than ever on this one.
The special's best character: **Maehara Shinobu**
She basically steals the show from Naru and Kei.
Average rating: **9.5**/10

---

### Post by nickless on 2005-08-23
As far as I know kenshin should also never have that much eps :D Bleach will probably have anext season, or there will be a complete new anime at least :)

---

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-23
> **nickless]As far as I know kenshin should also never have that much eps :D Bleach will probably have anext season, or there will be a complete new anime at least :)[/QUOTE]
I'd say at least 85% of everyone who saw the Rurouni Kenshin TV agrees with you on this.

[QUOTE=Animenewsnetwork.com Kenshin TV trivia]The anime follows the manga closely **until** the end of Kyoto Arc (episode 62). Here the manga continued and finished with the Revenge Arc, and the anime followed several smaller Arcs, such as the Christian Arc. The reason for this was simply because the anime had caught up with the manga in the end of Kyoto Arc, but the fans wanted more so the series didn't wait for the writer to finish the manga.[/QUOTE] 
[QUOTE=Rurouni Kenshin's wiki page]The television series and manga series of Rurouni Kenshin up to the end of the Kyoto Story Arc had been nearly identical in terms of plot with only minor differences. However when the television series caught up to the manga in terms of story the producers of the Rurouni Kenshin anime decided to diverge from Nobuhiro Watuski's manga and create their own story and characters. This led to the last arc of Rurouni Kenshin manga, the "Jinch&#363 said:**
>  If the Bleach creators forget about the quality their giving the audience and start making a gazillion episodes just for the monetary aspect of it, the anime's appeal can and will degrade over time.

[QUOTE=Rurouni Kenshin's wiki page]*Kenshin's original Japanese dialogue contains some unusual words which can cause problems in translation. Most of the time, he refers to himself with the extremely humble pronoun "sessha" (translated by Viz as "this one") and uses the formal verb "de gozaru" (conveyed by MediaBlasters by sentence-final phrases like "...that it is"). He shares this vocabulary with some characters in other series, such as Goemon Ishikawa in Lupin III. He also addresses most women with an honorific that was generally reserved for feudal lords; the translation "Miss Kaoru" does not really express the same degree of extreme courtesy as "Kaoru-dono". When in Batt&#333;sai persona, Kenshin stops being so polite; "de gozaru" disappears, and "sessha" is replaced with the more typical brash male pronoun "ore". Kaoru is quick to catch onto this fact as is demonstrated after the Sait&#333; fight. Yet another reason to watch it in the original language ;) .

[CENTER][http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rurouni_Kenshin](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rurouni_Kenshin)[/CENTER]

---

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-24
[QUOTE=YourSurrogateGod][spoiler]
[color=white]Why is the story dragging on and on? They saved Rukia, wouldn't it be a good idea to leave the whole thing at an end? And what's up with Aizen being brought back to life in the story?

The anime simply has a story about Kira, Hinamori and Renji when they were younger. I don't get how it ties in with the bigger story. The whole series seems to be taking a trip down Idiocy Lane. I'll see how things progress though...[/color]
[/spoiler][/QUOTE]
[spoiler]
[color=white]
a) Aizen = Alive ?
Ok so here's the deal. Yes, Aizen is alive (hmm, earlier than expected). The way they brought him back wasn't really amusing nor shocking, it's just plain out silly. So how is he alive? The body we all saw was his Soul Slayer **(...) **. His Soul Slayer has the power to sort of hiptonize whoever sees it (he had actually shown a small part of that "trick" to the lieutenants/Second-in-command). Besides that, it has the capability to take any form he desires, wich is why he made it look like his own corpse and make everyone believe he's dead. So since this kinda makes it lose balance, a thousand questions can be thrown here: So _no_ Shinigami was smart enough to get this ? Also, the Shinigamis didn't know what his powers were ? Didn't they ever get curious about his Soul Slayer or where it was when he was found dead (like, at least next to him or something)? Not even the elder Shinigami could figure it out ? So if you havent figured it out by now, Aizen is the bad guy and that weird Shinigami (The one with the weird eye brows/"no eyes") is his subordinate. By the way, this is just the tip of the _very_ confusing iceberg.

More confusing food for thought (no spoilers in this paragraph). The Shinigamis are  good guys right ? I mean, they one way or another fight evil right ? So that Shinigami with the poison bankai (trust me, there are a few more impressive bankais) Ishida nearly killed was really a good guy (since he had nothing to do with the plan) ? That would make Ichigo, Ishida and others the bad guys :? ?

It's really confusing the fact that between the Shinigamis there is such a HUGE amount of difference in their power. They all took more or less the same tests and none of them show any more respect for one Shinigami than another.. so its kind of confusing (maybe you still dont know why I'm saying this, but trust, its there).

You could be wondering so Aizen is the bad guy but WHY the rush to kill Rukia ? That's another story ..


b) Excess Baggage
"They saved Rukia"
Actually, she's not safe until the leaves Soul Society. Right now you don't know the importance of Rukia being executed so you may think there's no reason for them to chase them down, but trust me, they need Rukia.. badly.

To be Honest, I don't remember reading  so many backlashes one after the other, so I dont know what they showed, but if they showed why Hinamori (Aizen's chick right ? ..not sure) follows Aizen so much. Kira? He's not a key character anywhere so I don't know why they would have his flashbacks.. **excess filler** maybe ? I dont care how they try to make him or Hinamori look important, but they're just mere pawns in the plot. Now about Renji backlashes.. not sure either. We already know he loves Rukia dearly and he's give his life for her a long time ago. 
[/color]
[/spoiler]

Do you half understand now ?

### Post by sapo on 2005-08-28
woah... finally figured out how to have nice subtitles in ogm and mkv..

Totem was kinda ugly.. but now everything is pretty  :grin: 

MKV:
```
gmplayer -slang en
```

OGM:
```
gmplayer -slang English
```

I dont know why the OGM didnt work.. but i just used:

```
gmplayer -sid 0
```


The i just selected a nice font, size and border in the mplayer configuration... now my videos look like this:

[[IMG]http://img327.imageshack.us/img327/9450/mplayer6nh.th.jpg[/IMG]](http://img327.imageshack.us/my.php?image=mplayer6nh.jpg)

### Post by bored2k on 2005-08-30
[CENTER]**Naruto 01-22 Review**[/CENTER]

Ok I'll be honest. Naruto's start is not very promising, at all.  Although not really boring, the first 12-14 chapters aren't really that big of a deal and someone could simply say "I've seen enough, it's not worth it.". If you endure those not so good chapters, it really picks up after episode 14 or so (a friend, after I told him it gets awsome after so long just told me "... you have a lot of pacience). The action scenes get better, the plot thickens and everything gets more dramatic and dark. Besides that, the lighter episodes get a lot more funnier (Sakura even Naru-punches Naruto  :grin: ). If I would have seen just the first 10 episodes, I would have said that Naruto was just a childish attempt at creating a DBZ clone with another skin. 

I'll continue my Naruto reviews every once in a while ;).

P.S. - A lot of people would feel the same way I did, so I wonder if they are going to wait 2 months and a half to get the plot actually get somewhere (with one episode a week) (refering to the US release).[CENTER]

[IMG]http://img395.imageshack.us/img395/8296/sakura4nh.jpg[/IMG]
[IMG]http://img395.imageshack.us/img395/6328/gang6xb.jpg[/IMG][/CENTER]

### Post by bored2k on 2005-09-06
[CENTER]Picking up the leftovers:
**Naruto 26-54 review**[/CENTER]

I am glad I finally decided to stop everything and start on this one because I am so loving it right now. Sure, the first Season was not as good as expected (though It ended with a very good team battle sequence), but this one really picked up the pace for Season two with its Chuunin/ninja tests and its quite unexpected turns. To this point the most exciting and entertaining aspect of the anime is the vast character roster. Everyone is different, exciting and not boring at all (unlike a lot of series were you get 3 cool characters and like a dozen generic ones) to the point where my favorite characters aren't even the three main ones.

[CENTER][IMG]http://img93.imageshack.us/img93/9707/s8gd.jpg[/IMG] [/CENTER]
I am currently a few episodes into the beggining of Season 3, more precisely in the Closet Pervert/Pervert Sannin/Naruto training wich has been hilarious so far.

### Post by nickless on 2005-09-07
I have voted and voted and voted... and looked at my anime hints :D
Actually they were quite good, at least 10 animes I liked and just forgot to vote for appeared :) Anyway here my recommended top ten:
```

Place  	 Anime  	 Score  	 Votes
1.  	 Juuni Kokuki (26)  	 1000  	 25
2. 	Hagane no Renkinjutsushi (979) 	924 	32
3. 	Full Metal Panic? Fumoffu (959) 	896 	35
4. 	Planetes (895) 	852 	19
5. 	Koukaku Kidoutai STAND ALONE COMPLEX (247) 	851 	34
6. 	Hunter X Hunter (132) 	784 	18
7. 	Last Exile (581) 	734 	36
8. 	Elfen Lied (1544) 	724 	35
9. 	School Rumble (2165) 	721 	11
10. 	Genshiken (1783) 	717 	24
```
Most of them really look nice, some don't... ;)

And now Basilisk review:
I have seen now 1-8 and to be true if I hadn't already all eps until 16 on my hd, I wouldn't download any more. The story is tightening, seems that now it is going into a open battle between the two ninja clans. If I haven't said it already the story is pretty low, basically two ninja clans are fighting to decide who's going to be the next shogun. Each clan has 10 ninjas that have to be killed to win on a scroll. Score now is 9alive : 5alive  for the "bad" guys :) Hope that with ep9 there will be some serious action :D

### Post by nickless on 2005-09-08
Update: Basilisk up to ep 13 seen.
Tension was rising to the 8. episode and the most time was wasted on the primitive story, now there had to be some nice battle scenes or I'll quit, that was what I was thinking starting episode 9 and now I have to say this anime rocks :D
What I hoped in secret was right: The ninjas with the stupid and ridiculous techniques were just there to die and rise the tension between the two rival clans. Battle sequences are getting awesome and the techniques are really nice now :D
And Basilisk is NOT a mere ninja scroll copy (anymore ;) ) The Plot is really twisted and underlines the cruelety in  the fights. 
This will be the last review :) watch it yourself, even if the first eps are a bit of a hassle, this anime is really great and I have a feeling that it is going to get even better...

---

### Post by psychicdragon on 2005-09-08
I just watched the first 8 episodes of Naruto. I've heard from a lot of people that it's really cool etc. I liked the first episode.

There were definitely some good moments but I found myself getting pretty bored at times. Way too much extraneous dialogue. I know it's a kids show but they really spell things out for you. The part during the fight with the big-sword guy where Naruto took about 3 minutes to explain what just happened was retarded. It feels like I'm watching Pokemon at times, and Pokemon is generally more humorous.

Naruto is a pretty funny dude. The rest of the characters seem pretty typical, like Sasuke. Why does every anime need a brooding rival character? Sakura has done literally nothing but yell out "Sasuke-kun!" or "Naruto!" so far.

I'd probably like it more if I had never seen One Piece. All the characters on that show shine, especially Luffy.

I've got the first 30 episodes to watch, so I'm hoping things will start to get more interesting.

---

I guess the most of you live in america and so you don't have this problem, but here in germany it sucks hard :(
We are now on this anime wave and they actually translate anime and dub it german. I hate it. First dubbed anime I watchesd was Aika and I swear the guys who dub the animes are still the same :D and this was like 6 years ago...
I watched Trigun dubbed English and it really rocked, but most dubbed stuff I saw sucked, even if dubbed just English.
I know it is hard to translate animes to european languages and it is even harder to get it to the movement of the mouth, but damn sometimes it really gives me the creeps...
What do you think about dubbed anime today or in general?

---

### Post by darkoptix on 2005-09-08
There is some good dubbed anime, like Cowboy Bebop. I thought this was really well done. The voices suited the characters they portrayed. On the other hand, most anime is horribly dubbed. The best example of this that I can think off the top of my head would be Full Metal Panic. I watched in Japanese, and decided to watch with a couple of newbie anime friends. When previewing the first episode, Souske's voice totally did not fit the character that he was portraying. This just made the show seem retarded, and I couldn't handle it. The other voices also did not seem right. This may have been because I watched in japanese, but whatever I was it made it horrible to watch.

I think I may be the first one to mention this, but Naruto is really overrated. It just might be because it's for children, but it really over simplifies everything. I got the same vibe from watching DragonBall Z. Everything takes forever to happen, and not in a good animated way. This is like they are reusing the same animation just to make the show last longer. I realised this on a episode in the teens, when naruto is fighting that other ninja kid with the dog. This made me really dislike the series, and I had to stop watching it.

---

### Post by bored2k on 2005-09-08
[QUOTE=nickless]dubbing frenzy...
I guess the most of you live in america and so you don't have this problem, but here in germany it sucks hard :(
We are now on this anime wave and they actually translate anime and dub it german. I hate it. First dubbed anime I watchesd was Aika and I swear the guys who dub the animes are still the same :D and this was like 6 years ago...
I watched Trigun dubbed English and it really rocked, but most dubbed stuff I saw sucked, even if dubbed just English.
I know it is hard to translate animes to european languages and it is even harder to get it to the movement of the mouth, but damn sometimes it really gives me the creeps...
What do you think about dubbed anime today or in general?[/QUOTE]
 They all suck. Some of there are known to be excellent (Trigun and Cowboy Bebop) while most of them plain out suck. The biggest problem is the fact that Japanese can NOT be easily translated. You can dub an english movie to spanish or whatever 10x easier than japanese. When you dub japanese, you lose an enormous amount of cultural aspects of it and the fact that japanese and chinese has a lot of unique words, when it comes to translating, the dubber hast to really come up with a word that doesn't sound too out of place and that the viewer could just ignore without knowing what he just missed. This leads t o the interpretation problem (you basically get what the dubber perceives). Another aspect is the voice. Japanese voice actors tend to excel when it comes to voicing. They are rough when they need to, yet they get soft as a baby when it is demanded of them. Yet another problem? For some reason, the dubbers use what I call "the perfect english/spanish/_insert.language_ when it comes to doing their job. Why do originally foul mouthed characters speak so nicely in english? Because that is their way of wideing the range of viewers that is already outlined in the anime's plot and references. Why do teens speak like they are elite language academy teachers instead being, well.. teens? I could go on forever about this. Of course, there are some that interpreted or not sound and feel great/native, but only the minority of the hired dub groups can accomplish this.

*** Examples:**
1. Dragon Ball Z.
In anime character names usually have real meanings and connotations wich 95% of the time get lost. Why did Kaiou-Sama get the name King Kai in english? Didn't they know Kaiou-Sama means Honorable Priest/Elder?! That's a cultural aspect english only viewers will never perceive. This is part of what makes japanese so hard to translate. They have a very flexible and complex vocabulary depending on the person and moment. That's just one DBZ errata.

2. Continuing what I started in #1, several times anime characters simply refer to the other as "senpai" wich could refer to master or elder. This normally gets cut out. In english It simply doesn't sound right for a little girl to refer as an older person as "master" for no reason. One would just say "wth?" or "is this hentai or what?".

3. Name-sama, Nii-sama, Name-kun et al. Different sufixes that denote the person's respect or lack of respect to the other person. Where do these go? Did I mention japanese is not easily translated?

I am going to stop before I hurt my keyboard any more.

That's my two cents on the matter.

---

### Post by bored2k on 2005-09-08
[QUOTE=darkoptix]I think I may be the first one to mention this, but Naruto is really overrated. It just might be because it's for children, but it really over simplifies everything. I got the same vibe from watching DragonBall Z. Everything takes forever to happen, and not in a good animated way. This is like they are reusing the same animation just to make the show last longer. I realised this on a episode in the teens, when naruto is fighting that other ninja kid with the dog. This made me really dislike the series, and I had to stop watching it.[/QUOTE]
First of all I don't think this is a children's anime. It may be a Youth+12 or something, but certaintly not for children. They tend to touch certain subjects like mental violence, masturbation(twice) and others that all the violence in DBZ can not surpass. Although it gets kinda disturbing that they kinda take longer than usual to do things, as long as they keep you intrigued it should be Ok. It should be noted that it is based off a very large and succesful manga, so it is only understeandable that they want to cash in and not rush it up. Fillers are a bitch yeah, but its not a 25 eps series. We just have to cope with those.

### Post by bored2k on 2005-09-10
[QUOTE=nickless]I was in the naruto fever for some time, watched till ep70 or so. In no other show I have seen so many fillers :D It really is too much. Don't remember why I stopped, but now that some time has passed I can say that naruto isn't that good of an anime and ,yes, that it is somehwat childish sometimes. But damn when it comes to tension and holding their fans, even with way too much fillers, no anime surpasses naruto. When you cought the naruto fever you just can't stop :D[/QUOTE]
 The problem with Naruto is that due to the fact that they might overlap the manga, they show TOO DARN many backlashes! I mean, I SAW the damn episode, why do I wanna see a scene again? And if you are to show me backlashes, dont throw me some meaningless ''when they were childs'' ones! Also, when tension rises near the end, they spend about 3 minutes with no dialog, just staring at each other. That is not cool. Other than that, I like Naruto.

And by the way, I disagree on Naruto being that makes you hold your breath for the next episode the longer. Sure episodes are cool but its not like you don't know Naruto is not going to die or that some Jounin level is gonna step in or something -.- . You want tension? You want nail bitting? And Fullmetal Alchemist was born. Even when I was downloading it, waiting over an hour for the next episode was just TOO much. At times I found myself at 4am still waiting on episodes because I just couldnt let go of it. Characters die. Not in a cute and meaningless way, the way dramatically, very. Plot changes. A lot. Emotional pain is as present as ever (don't give me that Gaara crap.. he's cool but he ain't the Elric Brothers or any Alchemist for that matter).

I'm not dissing Naruto, I'm just squeezing in some FMA love :-P. I'm on episode 74 I believe.. the Konoha war is great. SO many characters onstage is cool. Can't wait for the unfolding. *Thinks FMA* .. Yeah, I could wait :P

### Post by YourSurrogateGod on 2005-09-14
[QUOTE=bored2k]YSG, what is Bleach anime up to nowadays ? (just so I know how much they've advanced)  What is the next episode supposedly gonna bring ? I'm having good times with Naruto and -yesterday's- Advent Children.[/QUOTE]
Well, here's a short overview...
[spoilers][color=white]
Well, in 48, you find out that Kuchiki's execution is moved to next day and Renji starts training for his bankai. The little captain kid (from the 10th division) fights Ichimaru Gin and nearly beats him if it weren't for a little dirty trick that Ichimaru plays on him and allows him to escape.

In 49, Rukia has a flash-back when she first joined the 13th division. She gets treated like a normal person by Kaien-dono (Ganju's older brother) and she begins to like him (as a friend.) We find out that the guy is married and that his wife is taken control of by a hollow. He goes after a hollow and is taken over himself. At which point he attacks Rukia and she kills him in self-defense. Rukia now carries quite a bit of guilt from that day on about what she did.
[/color][/spoilers]

### Post by psychicdragon on 2005-09-17
I just finished Naruto season 3. I can't believe the things I said about Naruto when I first started watching. Season 3 was so incredibly good. The fight scenes were all really cool, lots of characters getting involved, and the story is becoming much more interesting. I'm eagerly downloading season 4 right now.

Most of the characters have had at least a small chance to shine. Shikamaru in particular has really grown, he's also hilarious. I originally wasn't expecting too much from him. Kiba is like the only character who hasn't got to do much, I was pretty disapointed when he lost to Naruto in such a stupid fashion.

The fight between the Hokage and Orochimaru was so awesome. Easily the best part of the series so far. I'd say the Hokage is even more badass than Shaft.  :grin: 

About the only thing I've been doing for the last week is watching Naruto after I get home from school. My schedule goes like this: Wake up, school, food, Naruto, food, sleep, repeat  :grin:

### Post by bored2k on 2005-09-23
[CENTER]**[Blood +](http://anidb.info/perl-bin/animedb.pl?show=animelist&adb.search=blood%2B&x=0&y=0) **[/CENTER]

Just saw the trailer for Blood +, which is the name for the upcoming TV series based on [Blood: The Last Vampire](http://anidb.info/perl-bin/animedb.pl?show=anime&aid=43). Althought there's only thirty seconds, the visuals and animation looked quite good. 

> Throughout the passage of time and the shifts in backgrounds, a particular war continues to leave its mark on history, extending its influence into the modern world. There are two major groups involved in the ensuring war. The first group consists of monsters known as Chiropterans who can change their appearances into that of human beings. They are actually immortals who feed on the blood of the living. The second group is an organisation known as the "Red Shield", formed to track down these monsters and exterminate them. Otonashi Saya is a high-school girl who lives a peaceful life with her family. The only problem is, she has no recollections of her life beyond that of the past year. However, her happy life will be destroyed by an event that will lead her to her destined fate. > The Blood TV series has been scheduled for 6 pm Saturday on TBS starting in October. This is the prime time TV slot that has been previously given to extremely popular series such as Gundam Seed and Full Metal Alchemist.  The japanese broadcasting network TBS (not the Turner one) putting her hopes on it and giving it their elite time slot has to mean they are sure it will be good, or at least popular. I'll keep an eye out for this one when it gets released next month :D !.

[CENTER][IMG]http://img301.imageshack.us/img301/8381/17dx.jpg[/IMG] [/CENTER]

### Post by DJ_Max on 2005-09-24
If I could jump in a bit, with my unexperience in anime.

I've enjoyed watching DBZ over here in the US, but that was because thats all I knew, and pretty much all that was shown. Recently there has been an increase in anime on CartoonNetwork. My brothers watch TV more than I do, and are fond of Naruto, One Piece, and a few others. But due to me being programming and doing computer related stuff, I've had little time for TV. Also, for the simple fact, that the state of US TV is so bad (filled with horrible "reality shows"), I just don't watch it. Aside from some good Comedy Central shows, 24, The 4400, and of course, sports, most of TV is dreadful.

Then comes this thread, which I should have clicked a long time ago. I now have something to watch. Besides, DBZ, In the past II've watched a whole series of Gundam, I forgot which one, but I liked it. There was another anime (movie possibly) I've seen, again, the name escapes me, but it was about a bounty hunter (I believe female), but I enjoyed it. Other then those, I've watched very little anime. But thanks to this thread, I now have to replan my new computer with a bigger HD, and possibly RAID1.

So far, I've downloaded, and watched Bleach episodes (1-4), and Suzuka (1-2). I have to admit I'm really enjoying Bleach. Suzuka is a show I can see myself watching more of as well. But I see I have quite a few more episodes to go with Bleach. But my HD is fairly small, since I never had a need for extra space.

### Post by bored2k on 2005-09-26
I understand what you mean, but that's my point. They try to explain things that aren't really interesting by adding certain elements that just gets us confused in another direction. I didn't want to use this to compare it to Naruto, but now that I've seen 108 episodes of it, I think I'm capable of doing so. While certain things in Bleach are kinda vague and characters don't get the right introduction/attention or focus as we would want them to, in Naruto that's hardly the case. If there  is anything I appreciate in it is the fact that they try to encompass every single aspect of it (without the fillers, that is). YSG, you're reading the manga, I am reading it too. By the end of the "Saving Private Rukia" arch we're left pretty much clueless about who the new characters are and their story, which makes them all look kind of like a filler-like characters, when they certaintly should not be. I wished they would focus on the things that really makes us scratch our heads at times.

### Post by Chii on 2005-09-30
Wow.... big thread first off, lol.

Hope Im not posting too late, but as this was labeled the "** Official Otaku Thread**" I figured that I might as well post in here.

Now Im not an otaku perse in the sense that one would be obsessed with anime, but I do really enjoy to watch it.

Ive watched a few series all the way through.

My first and still one of my favorites if you cant tell is Chobits, hehe. Ive also seen:
Kiddy Grade
Avenger
Wolfs Rain
Cowboy Bebop
Battle Angel Alita 
Escaflowne
Gits SAC - parts of it
and am working on watching Full Metal Alchemist.

I also enjoy to read the various manga, having started with many of the series written by Clamp... Chobits, Wish, Four Leaf Clover (I believe this was written by them), I am currently reading Psychic Academy and Card Captor Sakura.

Now as Id said before, I am not completely obsessed with anime, but I do love it for many reasons.  
1) The drawing styles and various aspects to it can be very beautifully done.
2)  Anime as a genre is more like a series of animated movies rather than just cartoons, in that they cover everything from Comedy, to Tragedy with absolutely every genre you can think of in between, many times even going so far as to mix these elements within some of the series. 
3)  Watching anime in a group can be a very very fun social activity when you have the right people together :D.  
And finally, 
4)  For me at least and Im sure for others (perhaps not immediately in this community)  accessibility in the way of subtitling. Be it fan-subbing or something more professionally done.  Because due to an accident in 2000 I am unable to hear, and so consequently my viewing choices are narrowed down.  And thus I was very pleased to find out that since most of the animes shown are in Japanese, subtitles are most often a must.

Well their are my .02 cents on the topic :P Thank you for taking time to read through it, hehe.

### Post by bored2k on 2005-10-05

By the way, if you watched up until episode 26 it means you saw the first of two seasons. Trust me, the second season picks up right where the first one left it and never goes down again (in terms of the "OMFG :-|" feeling). I **still** remember when I saw all the "outlaws" getting out of that hidden military base (*ie* their jail) together. That's when I said "Ok I have to stop everything else and focus on this.. if the climax can get any higher.. it will probably be the best anime I have ever seen". And yeah, it is the best anime I have ever seen.

The Wind of Homeland[/CENTER]

      I have to admit the first 13 chapters weren't that exciting at all. They were dull, the comedy aspect of them was not that good (way inferior than the one on [Full Metal Panic? Fumoffu]("http://anidb.info/perl-bin/animedb.pl?show=anime&aid=959")) and the action wasn't hot either, but after the start of 14th episode, scratch that. The three "The  Wind of Homeland" episodes were the most dramatic this mecha hater has probably ever seen. By the end of this anime, if it continues the pace of these three chapters, I would probably be moved enough has to get Gundam Seed (after Ghost in the Shell TV that is). And yes, these three chapters were **that **impressive.
[CENTER][IMG]http://img91.imageshack.us/img91/226/soka2gs.jpg[/IMG][/CENTER]

### Post by bored2k on 2005-10-06
[CENTER]**Full Metal Panic** 17-24 :
**Into the Blue**[/CENTER]

Great finale. This is one of those of the anime (this season at least) that starts really slow and not really giving you anything to grab on at first, but after a set of jaw dropping episodes (start with the "The Wind of the Homeland" batch) it keeps you thrilled 'til the end. I still have to say that episodes 15-17 were probably my favorite, but after those the series gets a lot better than the ones before episode 14. As someone who has never found mecha anime very appealing, this one turned out to be very fun and entertaining (probably to the factor that they shifted the attention off mecha a lot with the comedy aspect and the character relations in it).

[CENTER][IMG]http://img90.imageshack.us/img90/7577/dathindasamily4dr.jpg[/IMG][/CENTER]

**What's next in line? **[**Princess Mononoke**]("http://anidb.info/perl-bin/animedb.pl?show=anime&aid=7") and [**Ghost in the Shell: [color=darkblue]Stand Alone Complex[/color]**]("http://anidb.info/perl-bin/animedb.pl?show=anime&aid=247"). I'm really interested in watching Stand Alone Complex as I have heard nothing but greatness about it and also because I'd like to see what their take on mecha is and how the make it look appealing. Oh and by the way, I saw the first three minutes of the first episode and my jaw **dropped** to the floor and stayed there for the whole duration of the small scene I saw just to see how it looked.

In about three days I will have Stand Alone Complex here and I still haven't made up my mind on what to get next. I don't like acquiring unfinished anime so I'm still not looking at Full Metal Panic! The Second Raid, but maybe I'll get some of it or Ghost In the Shell: 2nd Gig, or who knows what I'll end up acquiring :-P.

---

### Post by YourSurrogateGod on 2005-10-22
WHOAA!!!!! FMP TSR 13 was teh awesome!!!! ( <- Yes, I'm a dork :p .)
Spoilers:
[color=white]Ok, just when Souske finds out from Gauron (sp?) that Chidori is dead and jumps out of the window of the building, he is confronted by his former comrades and is almost killed by one of them. This kinda blew my mind away. Fortunately Chidori comes to Hong Kong just in time to smack some sense into Sagara-kun (literally, she delivered major pwnage, I'd be scared too... could be on par with Naru.) Now, this was well timed, since he came and saved his commander from being killed by Amalgam's execution squad (the chinese girl piloting the Venom was pretty much doomed.) Now, Souske, busts out all of the stops and goes berserk on the 5 Venoms (yes folks, 5, he raped them.)

The thing that one noticed towards the end is that Sagara is displaying more emotions from his typical "Yes sir!" "No sir!" talk (although he continues to have some trouble.) That and he now calls Danaan's Captain Tessa instead of "Yes Captain!"... atleast when they're in private.

As the episodes progressed, it has become clear that Tessa *and* Chidori love this guy and since this has not been resolved by now, it appears that there will be some interesting stories in the future.

Unfortunately, I'm also under the impression that this will be the last TSR episode, which makes me sad : - ( (the thing was 27 minutes long as opposed to 23 and it didn't contain the intro and the ending music-video themes.) If I were to make a prediction, the next series of FMP will be more like Fumoffu... or atleast with more humor and touchy moments between Chidori and Souske (Tessa maybe?)[/color]

---

### Post by ubuntu27 on 2005-10-22
OMG !! I've never though that in a Linux forum someone will be talking about Anime and Manga :D

ANyway, Here is something that I recommend you guys to read. It is Called [DEATHNOTE](http://www.narutofan.com/plus/manga/series/deathnote.html). 

[http://manga.anime-source.com/manga/modules.php?name=Manga&id=10](http://manga.anime-source.com/manga/modules.php?name=Manga&id=10)


Ok, you can also download from here: [http://www.narutofan.com/plus/manga/series/deathnote.html](http://www.narutofan.com/plus/manga/series/deathnote.html)

Here is teh story:
Raito [Light] was the perfect high school kid. Good grades and a happy family. He was normal until he found a notebook. This notebook is owned by Shinigami and it allows the user to kill people by writing the person's name in it. Discovering this, Raito decided to use it to clean the world off criminals.

Thus, criminals slowly began to die one by one...........

Eventually people began to notice this. How could criminals all suddenly die by the same cause!? And at the same time? Thus, government of countries decide to catch this mysterious criminal killer. Headed by the mysterious detective L, a group of cops are out searching for Raito.......

### Post by bored2k on 2005-11-02
Ok, after watching the first two episodes of Full Metal Panic! The Second Raid, I just wanted to say that this is one of the most beautiful (could even be on top of my list) anime  I have ever seen. It's far from being uber flashy like say GITS SAC or Macross Zero, but it shows the viewer what they want in the most gratious ways. Drawings are crisp, clear and beautiful, and on top of that, what IMO really excels is the animation. Everything moves just as (if not  more) smooth than a regular _non japanese_ cartoon in a way that demans attention and gaze. Besides all that, after watching several anime after FMP? Fumoffu and the first FMP!, the voice acting is superb. I am so loving this anime a LOT more than its prequel! (I'm not counting Fumoffu that much because although it deals with the same characters, it's 100% submerged into comedy). The jokes are feel a lot better than the previous ones too. 

I don't know if it'll be on my top anime list for sure by the time I finish it, but I know it's on my top 5 of most beautiful anime ever. And one thing I am already certain is this: best mecha anime I've seen (for those who don't know, I'm not a fan of mecha... at all).

### Post by darkoptix on 2005-11-07
I just started watching Noir. 

Mireille Bouquet is a professional assassin, and a very good one at that. But when she follows up an e-mail from a young Japanese girl named Yumura Kirika, inviting her to take "a pilgrimage to the past", her life becomes even more dangerous than it already is. Now, with a haunting melody invoking the memory of an event long past, Mireille and Kirika decide to work together to find the truth about a thousand year old organization that has controlled both of their lives since before they were born. And the only clue in their search, the only thing Kirika remembers about herself, becomes their working codename: a name designating an ancient fate, of two maidens who reign over death--Noir.
[http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=407]("http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=407")

I am on ep 10ish, and so far it is a alright series. Seems a bit to monotone, and weird, but I am going to finish it.

### Post by bored2k on 2005-11-07
I just posted this in the KAA forum, but since it's kind of my small review of FMP! TSR, here it is:

[QUOTE=bored2k]KAA has done it once again! I can't believe DVDs are coming out already!

For those who are not sure if re-downloading or just starting with KAA while waiting for their releases, let me tell you, you don't get any better than KAA. I already have arguably the best complete release out there and I am definitely getting KAA's and trashing the old one.

Here's a comparison of how much KAA's owns the "best" one out there:
**KAA**   ---> [http://kaa.animeconnection.net/torrentpage/pix/fmptsr05.png](http://kaa.animeconnection.net/torrentpage/pix/fmptsr05.png)
**Other** ---> [http://img99.imageshack.us/img99/7473/screenshot05cj.png](http://img99.imageshack.us/img99/7473/screenshot05cj.png)

**FMP! TSR is breathtaking. **Action scenes rock, animation is smooth and mostly because the characters deliver MASSIVE ownage! The old ones rock, and I have to say I don't think I've seen such a wonderful set of new ones. Gates (voiced by the same guy who gives life to Jiraiya in Naruto) is just **completely** *mal à la tête* and the two sisters are just... KA (for Kick Ass :D). Also, it is my opinion that this series has the best ensemble of adversaries for an anime. 

TSR is **definitely** in my top 5 favorite anime (Note: I don't even like much of the mecha genre besides FMP!/? and Eva).[/b][/i][/QUOTE]

### Post by darkoptix on 2005-11-19
Planetes Review
[http://www.animenfo.com/animetitle,1244,xkqkfo,planet_es.html]("http://www.animenfo.com/animetitle,1244,xkqkfo,planet_es.html")
Alright, I just finished watching all the episodes of this series, and overall I really enjoyed it.

> It is the year 2075. In a world where travelling between Earth, Space stations and the moon has become normal.

Hachimaki (Hoshino Hachirouta) is just an ordinary salaryman. He works at a space space station collecting debris. However, he has a dream of owning his very own space ship someday. And so he is spending his days collecting debris together with his colleagues Yuri, Fii and his friend Tanabe. Now it is his fourth year doing that work and he is going to be 25 years old soon, which is why he starts to think about his life.

How does Hachimaki feel? Well, today, Hachimaki is going to do the same job...

Although I usually like action and mindfucks for anime, this series really touched me. I felt that it could show what the future of space development could be. The characters seemed well done, and the animation was alright, but no GITS or FMP:TSR. The story is where this series shines. It was quite well put together.

In the end, I would recommend to watch if you are into Sci-Fi dramas.

Now to finish Noir...

---

### Post by bored2k on 2005-11-19
[CENTER]**[Witch Hunter Robin]("http://anidb.info/perl-bin/animedb.pl?show=anime&aid=96")**
Episode 01: Replacement

[img]http://img292.imageshack.us/img292/2572/whoa11zs.jpg[/img][img]http://img292.imageshack.us/img292/5509/whoa0vf.jpg[/img]
[/CENTER]

Whoa, talk about good first impression. Dark and serious tone, impressive visuals, slow but intriguing and tasteful pace, superb [incidental music]("http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/lexicon.php?id=74") plus a good cast (nothing over the top from any character in this episode) with a silent yet intriguing main character, I think I will like this one a lot. Like the [ANN review]("http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/reviews/display.php?id=541") said, it's a sobre anime for the "discerning anime fan with high-minded taste, above the usual pabulum that endlessly crowds the shelves".

I said a lot just by the first episode huh? Well, it was *that* good.

### Post by wishyjr on 2005-11-24
ok guys question...

ive just finished watching GITS:SAC. Cracking series!! but was anyone else a little let down by the way Section9 suddenly seemed to come back to life? I mean, the hacker guy (Ishikawa?) got a bit of a hiding from that soldier in the back of the van -was he actually killed there and then? what about Borma? wasnt he followed out of the sewers buy about four guards then suddenly loads of rounds are blapped off!!

I dunno, i think the creators of the series could ahve done with just a little more elaboration on the three months after the unit was disbanded -anyone agree?

Beautiful series though -if anyone hasnt seen it, really, make an effort to do so. Fantastic!!

---

### Post by bored2k on 2005-11-24
[QUOTE=wishyj]was anyone else a little let down by the way Section9 suddenly seemed to come back to life?[/QUOTE] Yes. Personally, I think the best way this series could have ended would have been in the death or at least complete separation of the whole Section 9. Of course, this would have blocked any further attempts to milk the Ghost in the Shell (Ghost in the Shell 2 movie and the second season), but it would have been a lot more **coherent** and **shocking** than the whole team doing the Hollywood's century-old "Big explosion && Miraculously respawned (unscratched)".

Also, the last episode with Togusa almost literally going nuts because of his solitude was one of the most boring and non creative episodes of the season. It was pretty much an episode to create a link between the second season and the second movie (Innocence). Of course, the exact given to us was that Aramaki set everything up to save the team even after they're public disbandment, but that just felt **so** out of place.

I haven't acquired/seen the GITS:SAC second reason mostly because after that ending I don't really feel in the mood for watching it (let down). I'm going to do so eventually (probably start in about 2 weeks) though.

**Final thoughts:** Why don't media producers (anime, music, movies, etc) learn from anime like Cowboy Bebop on when and how to **stop** a series before we get disappointed by their efforts to milk it even more?

---

### Post by bored2k on 2005-12-02
[CENTER]**The *clean* greatness of Fullmetal Alchemist**[/CENTER]

From [The AnimeNewsNetwork Answerman]("http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/columns/answerman.php?id=224"):[QUOTE=Someone w/a question]"I went to a website that said that Trigun had a lot of filler episodes and they did not mention anything about Fullmetal Alchemist or Cowboy Bebop
having any filler episodes. Does Cowboy Bebop and Fullmetal Alchemist have filler episodes?"[/quote]
[quote=The ANN Answerman]
Response: Depends on your definition of "filler", I suppose. Most people consider "filler" to be one of two things: either they're episodes that don't follow the manga storyline, or they're episodes that don't have anything to do with the main plot. By that standard, most of Cowboy Bebop is "filler" since there is an arching plot but only 6 episodes or so actually deal with it; the rest of them are standalone stories that serve to develop the characters a bit.

Trigun does indeed have a lot of filler since the story doesn't really kick in until the last half of the show, **but Fullmetal Alchemist, to my recollection, *doesn't have a single episode* that isn't dedicated solely to the main plot.**[/QUOTE]FMA is just ... unique.

### Post by bored2k on 2005-12-03
Just finished watching [Witch Hunter Robin]("http://anidb.info/perl-bin/animedb.pl?show=anime&aid=96"). It's a very cool and dark anime. Certaintly wouldn't recommend it for viewers who need a big splash every 180 seconds, but to the rest of us who don't necessarily need it, it's very good. It has a few boring downtimes every few episodes, but overall it's good. Based on [AniDB's voting system]("http://www.anidb.net/wiki/index.php?title=Votes:_Anime"), I give it an 8/10.

> **8 - Very good**
This anime is better than good, there are a few moments you were very impressed by it and every anime fan should watch it. However, it is still not great as it still has a few points to improve.

### Post by bored2k on 2005-12-07
[center][**Genshiken**]("http://anidb.info/perl-bin/animedb.pl?show=anime&aid=1783")

[img]http://dongato.dyndns.org/imgtmp/awards/COMEDY_GENSHIKEN2.jpg[/img][/center]

Very good piece of anime. It has some very high points which are both tremendously fun and "educational", while there are others that are kind of bland and not as fun (of course, we have to remember it's an anime based on the life of a group of otakus, not as proportionally blown up as other "short comic burst" anime like FMP? Fumoffu). I was kind of disappointed of the ending of it because the last episodes were in my opinion the less comic ones. Although those of us who enjoy anime might find it good, this anime will definitely **not** get more people interested in anime. It's also good to point out that this anime does a tremendously good job at dealing with about a dozen different issues/problems teh hardcore otaku would have in a funny way.
I rated it an **8/10**.

I think the Madarame Harunobu character really deserved the [Best Male Character 2004]("http://forum.anime-reactor.net/viewtopic.php?t=13803"). Yes, above Ed Elric. And even though I haven't seen Elfen Lied, It would be hard to beat Kasukabe Saki as the Best Female Character 2004.

[center][IMG]http://dongato.dyndns.org/imgtmp/awards/MALE_JURY_GENSHIKEN_3.jpg[/IMG][IMG]http://dongato.dyndns.org/imgtmp/awards/FEMALE_JURY_GENSHIKEN_2.jpg[/IMG][/center]

For the record, although I enjoyed it, I barely felt related to the anime.

### Post by bored2k on 2005-12-12
[QUOTE=wishyjr]what is the basic plot for read or die? ive sen some trailers for it but it doesnt really give the viewer an idea of what the plot is about. :) cheers.[/QUOTE]> A little into the future from now. A few years after the army`s "Human Selection Campaign".

In a street somewhere in Hong Kong, there was a detective office. The name of it was "The Three Sisters Detective Company". The 3 beautitful sisters there were working day and night to settle an incident regarding a book. The girls` names are Anita, Maggy and Michelle.

The oldest sister Michelle who likes all kinds of books, Maggie and even the youngest sister Anita who doesn`t like adult books, in truth possesses the special power of a "paper user".

Using and not using their powers, what will happen to the cases they get?
[QUOTE=wishyjr]

### Post by bored2k on 2005-12-21
[QUOTE=wishyjr]ok since i kinda killed the conversation, heres one...

since its christmas, tokyo godfathers. seen it? what do you guys make of it, personally i liked it. Also what other xmas themed anime are there out there? thoughts ladies n gentlemen?[/QUOTE]

Yes, I really liked it.

As of Tokyo Godfathers, no, I haven't seen it. Is it that good? I didn't even know it was based on Christmas.

### Post by bored2k on 2006-01-05
[QUOTE=otake-tux]Is torrenting really ilegal as a whole?
I know thatif its a licensed anime it brings up issues but as far as I know, the debate as to wether fansubs and torrentin are ilegal or not is still up in the air.[/QUOTE]
[http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/lexicon.php?id=63](http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/lexicon.php?id=63)
> Although technically illegal -- Japanese copyrights are honored in the US and other Berne Convention countries and vice versa -- some anime licensors look the other way on fansubs as "free advertising" since people who see a fansub are likely to buy the release on DVD if they enjoy it. Fansubbers also follow a code of honor of sorts, and halt all work on a title once it has been licensed in the US.Fansub is a really deep term. [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fansub](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fansub)

### Post by bored2k on 2006-01-05
[CENTER]**School Rumble OVA review** 

[IMG]http://img203.imageshack.us/img203/5923/screenshot03bt.png[/IMG] [IMG]http://img210.imageshack.us/img210/4397/screenshot18yo.png[/IMG] [/CENTER]

Greatness. I think it does a good job at making us want more of the series, as well as introduce us to two of the new characters and save some series time by introducing the year on this OVA (ie with who is everyone with, where is everyone sitting in the classroom, etc). Although I didn't find the second episode's Prehistoric Rumble part, overall the OVA is quite good. One thing worth mentioning is how **awsome** and well done were the visual and sound effects. My jaws dropped a few times over usually generic effects as purple "I'm mad/sad" smoke and such. Anyone who loves School Rumble needs to see this one. Anyone who hasn't seen School Rumble, simply put, needs to.

### Post by bored2k on 2006-01-10
[CENTER]**Naruto manga 234-290: Post anime's 34 (and counting) fillers**[/CENTER]

So today, after finding out that everything after Naruto anime chapter 135 ([169-135] that's about 34 **straight** filler episodes), I decided to pick up the manga where I left the anime (which was on manga chapter 234). So after starting my reading, I quickly got hooked. In short, in just one day, I caught up to the manga which is [290-234=Ans * 19] ~1,000 pages.

What's in stock in Naruto's main storyline? **Greatness.** I'm not sure if any of you like reading the manga or prefer waiting for the anime, but trust me, you want to see this one. Also, just as Bleach's manga, I believe its a lot better than the anime. **Why?** Well, besides it not being as afraid to be graphic as the anime (In the anime when Shikamaru falls into the Sound's Four illusion, his arm just gets lossy, while on the manga, his arm melts to the point where his bones are visibly damaged), mainly because it's condensed. There's barely no (if any) completely off-topic fillers, and the annoying and long recaps are kept at a minimum. Like the wiki explains,

> With weekly releases, the manga currently has 290 chapters (only 78 of which have been released in the U.S.). The anime currently has 167 episodes. Although it debuted some time after the manga, the anime is quickly catching up since one anime episode usually covers around two manga chapters. **To prevent overlapping**, the anime's producers tend to organize content from the manga chapters into long, uneventful sections followed by short bursts of energy-packed action, sometimes adding filler. However, the anime generally remains true to the manga.

---

### Post by skydvrgrl on 2006-01-26
Well seeing as you grew up around mech based anime (like me :D) I would suggest Gundam Seed, then Gundam Seed Destiny to begin with. Inuyasha is also a good one and easy to get into.

Personally I am getting into Naruto now and cant say enough good things about it!! :D

### Post by bored2k on 2006-01-26
[QUOTE=skydvrgrl]Well seeing as you grew up around mech based anime (like me :D) I would suggest Gundam Seed, then Gundam Seed Destiny to begin with. Inuyasha is also a good one and easy to get into.

Personally I am getting into Naruto now and cant say enough good things about it!! :D[/QUOTE]Ok. Naruto. It's good, but you don't want it to be your re-introductory anime, considering how fillerish it can get (filler being dozens and dozens of chapters who have no point other than stretch the series enough to make the writers think about what they're going to do next, ie let the manga advance).

Yeah, I'll agree with my man the frogman (that's sapo for all you others), Fullmetal Alchemist would be a great place to start. It has almost every genre imaginable, and it uses all of them greatly. Mecha? It's there. Romance? Platonic, but it's there. Drama? Don't get me started. Action? I ain't even gonna say nothing on it. 51 episodes of enjoyment, trust me.

Here would be my three anime "startup" runners:
[LIST=1]
[*][Fullmetal Alchemist]("http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=2960") (because it is _the_ anime)
[*][Cowboy Bebop ]("http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=13")(there's no arguing this one)
[*][Ghost in the Shell: Stand Alone Complex]("http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=910") (this one's a huge eye-opener for both anime fans, and nots)
[*][Monster ]("http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=3750")(but _only_ if you're into mystery/dramatically-intense movies!; if you do... get ready for some deep shizznit)
[/LIST]Note: If you're seriously into mecha or want to start there, I couldn't think of an anime that's better at being shockingly amazing, with its action-packed lose style that's Full Metal Panic, most notably [Full Metal Panic! The Second Raid]("http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=4449") (despite its name, the third season after FMP? v1 and FMP Fumoffu!). I'm not a fan of mecha (at all... except for Evangelion... an anime that's definitely not for green anime viewers), and FMP TSRaid turned out to be one of my favorite anime ever (in my top 5.... with only 13 chapters).

### Post by bored2k on 2006-01-27
[QUOTE=bloodborne]So the arc Bleach is starting to take now is in fact a filler?[/QUOTE]
[QUOTE=bleachblog.com]The Bleach **64** episode from Bleach anime will be released tomorrow, and will be entitled: “New School Term, Renji has come to the Material world?!” That’s right, the **beginning of the** Bleach anime **filler** with this Bleach 64 Episode.[/QUOTE]
Sorry guys, but it's the truth. 

Only to consolate you guys, trust me, without a **huge** (and I mean a **huge**) filler arch, the series would overlap the manga, and we all know what happens when anime overlap the original manga and try to invent stuff just to continue (hint hint: Rurouni Kenshin's 3rd season, ie the abysmal fall of Kenshin). The Bleach anime is arguably faster at catching up to the manga than even Naruto itself, so expect a few fillers every once in a while. Honestly, I expect an entire season of fillers. That's right boys and girls, a full year, ie 24-26 episodes of filler episodes, much like Naruto is right now. In the case of Naruto, it's greatly needed to let the manga advance, mainly because after naruto anime episode 134 [the end of the Sasuke retrieval arch], the series takes a incredibly huge leap in greatness.

### Post by Stormy Eyes on 2006-01-30
OK, I need a new series. It has to fit the following criteria:

1. Fansubs are available.
2. It has not been licensed in North America.
3. There should be no more than 2 filler episodes out of 26.
4. The ending must make sense. My wife doesn't want to put over 10 hours into watching a series only to be cheated by a shitty ending that only makes sense if you're Japanese.

My wife likes Cowboy Bebop, Bleach, Hellsing, Saiyuki, RahXephon, Slayers, Fullmetal Alchemist, Vampire Hunter D, and Bastard!!, if that helps.

### Post by bored2k on 2006-01-30
[QUOTE=Stormy Eyes]OK, I need a new series. It has to fit the following criteria:

1. Fansubs are available.
2. It has not been licensed in North America.
3. There should be no more than 2 filler episodes out of 26.
4. The ending must make sense. My wife doesn't want to put over 10 hours into watching a series only to be cheated by a shitty ending that only makes sense if you're Japanese.

My wife likes Cowboy Bebop, Bleach, Hellsing, Saiyuki, RahXephon, Slayers, Fullmetal Alchemist, Vampire Hunter D, and Bastard!!, if that helps.[/QUOTE]With those four requirements, to me it would be hard to recommend a new series, mostly because most of the _good_ anime I know of are already licensed, and those who are not licensed have not yet finished, which makes it impossible for us to say if the ending makes sense or not. Ignoring the "must not be licensed" requirement, here are some you/she might lvie:

[LIST]
[*][Monster]("http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=3750"). Monster. The story is so good New Line Cinema bought the rights to make a live-action movie. One of the most succesful manga in Japan, the adult viewer is sure to get a kick out of it. Just... read the plot, you'll love it. Oh. and the anime is **not** licensed.
[*]Liking the FMAlchemist type (_broad_ fantasy) inmediately makes me think you/she might like [Scrapped Princess]("http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=2083"). Don't let the name&&silly looking plot fool you, its extremely good. Almost too good. My favorite unfinished anime (which won't be unfinished for long, now that I got reminded about it :)). I'm not sure about the ending though, but my friends have told me its good.
[*]Hellsing, VHunterD reminds me of [Trinity Blood]("http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=4874"), which _just_ got licensed ([2005-07]("http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/article.php?id=6954")). AFAIK, it's not your regular vampire flick.[/LIST]

I don't think Monster will be unlicensed for long, so get it while it's hot.

### Post by bored2k on 2006-01-31
[CENTER]**Fullmetal Alchemist: **The Conqueror of Shambala

[IMG]http://img66.imageshack.us/img66/1622/fma9fd.jpg[/IMG][/CENTER]

Finally, after months of waiting for it, I got the chance to see it. Was I impressed? **yes**. I don't like spoiling things for others, so don't expect to get into any details at all. Is it rewarding? oh **yes**.

First off, itsAnimation: Beautiful. I can't really stress this one out much, but the animation was so clean and well done it had me in awe throughout the whole film, specially in the action sequences. itsMusic: On the mark. It just feels right. Never does it feel bothersome or innapropiate; it's just there when it is needed and with great quality. With the whole cast reprising their respective roles, I don't even need to mention how great of a job each one did. itsStory: mature. Although the FMA series isn't childish at all (*au contraire*, it has its share of _very_ deep and  intense moments), this one feels a lot more mature and adult. At one point, they even deal with the german Fuhrer (aka Adolf Hitler), and everyonce in a while it deals with nazis and such, so go figure. itsEnding: let's just say it won't leave your mouth dry. I can't say more without biasing any who's reading this and hasn't seen it.

If you have seen the FMA series, you owe this one to yourself. If you have not, you owe the FMA series to yourself. Beware: due to its great use of parallelisms, those who have not seen the series completely will probably miss and/or not understand most of it. Besides that, it would still feel like a shallow and not explicative film at all (just like the Blood/X movie feel like), so don't even try it unless you've seen and understood the series.

[QUOTE=Stormy Eyes]I wouldn't say it looks *bad*; it's just that the visual style is more naturalistic than is common for anime. It looks very gritty and realistic, but that sort of style doesn't appeal to everyone. I prefer a more stylized look myself, but I'm watching *Monster* for the story. If I want stylish, I'll go watch *Hellsing*.[/QUOTE]
Heh. To be honest, I don't really like Hellsing's visual style (btw, you do know there will be a [new OVA ]("http://anidb.info/perl-bin/animedb.pl?show=anime&aid=3296")out right?), and although It does aim to be real, I still don't see how anyone could appeal to it. In my opinion, it's a test for the viewers to see if they'd get biased by the visuals (I would). If you ever get interested in watching the Mac-daddy of stylish anime, you should check out [Gankutsuou: The Count of Monte Cristo]("http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=4194"). As you're able to read from its ANN reviews (  [1]("http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/reviews/display.php?id=852") and [2]("http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/reviews/display.php?id=934")), at first, it's so much it might even cause headaches (**trust me**, I tried watching that and my 3am-ish eyes could not bare it).

Note: That should also be a good anime to watch. I don't think one can go wrong with an Alexandre Dumas story. The ANN reviewer even labeled as *gasp* one of the greatest of all time.

### Post by qyot27 on 2006-02-16
After trying to read through the rest of the thread (I made through the first 210 posts), I've given up.

Anyway, let's see.  The first time I was exposed to anime (and actually *knew* what it was that I was watching) was back in '95 or '96, thanks to the Sci-Fi Channel's Saturday Anime feature; I also saw Sailor Moon that summer and DBZ and Samurai Pizza Cats that fall - I had seen G-Force when we first got Cartoon Network, but didn't know that it was anime at that point (and based off of G-Force, didn't see Eagle Riders as anime either when I saw it at the same time as DBZ - I swear, English releases of Gatchaman are confusing to follow).  I didn't really get further into it until the following Spring, when I watched Voltron and in early '98, Robotech, both thanks to the original incarnations of Toonami.  Saw Gundam Wing the next year, got back into DBZ and followed it through Cell, and then my interest fizzled off for another couple of years.

In Summer 2002, my interest was rekindled *big time*, partly due to my family getting broadband and me getting introduced to AMVs on P2P networks.  I quickly watched Evangelion and Ah! My Goddess, and when I got a job the next Spring I started actually buying DVDs.  Of course, since I haven't had a job for the last year and a half, my DVD collection has just about stopped growing.  I'm really feeling the rub because I'd rather edit my AMVs with DVD footage rather than rely on fansubs, etc.

I really need to catch up on my anime queue.  I have and need to watch/resume watching/start over on:

Ah! My Goddess TV
Bleach
Bokusatsu Tenchi Dokuro-chan
Fatal Fury (OVAs and movie; I've had it since 2003 and still haven't watched it)
King Of Fighters: Another Day
Fushigi Yuugi
Galaxy Angel X (after I finish watching the 3rd season)
Gall Force (all of it)
Gunbuster (the original OVA)
Gunbuster 2
Ichigo 100%
Itsudatte My Santa!
Jinki: Extend
Macross Plus (same deal as Fatal Fury)
Macross Zero
Mahou Sensei Negima
Mai-Hime
Mai-Otome
Mamotte Shugogetten (TV & OVA)
Rozen Maiden
Trinity Blood

There may be others on that list, but running through my huge stack of discs and DVDs didn't turn anything else up.  This isn't counting series that I want to watch but don't have yet, either.  College, multi-editor AMV projects, and my own procrastination kind of floored me last year.

In general, my tastes range in [practically] every direction, although I can honestly say that even though I like most of the anime I end up watching (possibly due to me only watching anime I think I'll like), it is very hard for me to stand watching Weiß Kreuz.

### Post by Stormy Eyes on 2006-02-16
[QUOTE=bored2k]Really? Is it as good as the reviews I've read say (Animenewsnetwork.com reviews says it's one of the best ever)?[/quote]

Yeah, it was damned good. My wife loved it.

[QUOTE=bored2k]How much Dumas butchering goes on there?[/quote]

Gonzo certainly took some liberties with the material, but some deviation is to be expected when you use a science fiction setting, make Albert de Morcerf the protagonist instead of Edmond Dantes. I wouldn't say that Gonzo butchered the material. In fact, I think Dumas might have approved of *Gankutsuou*.

I still find the original novel more satisfying, as it explores the limits of human justice and illustrates both the humanizing bonds of love and what can happen to a man when he loses all ties of affection. After all, it was Haydee who melted the Count's heart and reminded him that he was still human and could still love.

[QUOTE=bored2k]Didn't your eyes bleed with so many shifts of colors (I watched the 5-minute "preview"... boy was that intense).[/quote]

I didn't find the color shifts a problem, but *Gankutsuou* is one anime I will *not* watch while tripping on acid.

### Post by bored2k on 2006-02-16
By the way, here's what anime-planet recommends to those who like it:
[http://www.anime-planet.com/animerec/888.html](http://www.anime-planet.com/animerec/888.html)

Note: Juuni Kokuki. If you decide for that one, I have a word of advice: patience. It's not the regular "omg anime" which ends every one of its episodes with a *gasp* ending. It slowly develops on you. I've watched quite a bit of it, and it's quite deep. I still haven't got to the point where I'm "omg" by it though.

Note 2: Although it has 45 episodes, it was originally supposed to be **way** bigger than that, so if you don't like to feel like you just wasted 17.3 hours of your life for a lousy ending, skip it.

### Post by bored2k on 2006-02-16
> **Protostar]I LOVE Dragonball Z and Naruto. I'm tired of everyone hating on DBZ. I cannot understand why its so disliked by many. BTW, isn't manga anime porn?[/QUOTE]
Protostar, you just made me smack my forehead. Hard. Anyways, let's start. 

There is no such thing as "manga anime". There's manga, then there's anime.
> Manga (&#28459 said:**
> 
[quote]Anime (&#12450;&#12491;&#12513;) is a style of cartoon animation originating in Japan. Anime is characterized by character and background styles which may be created by hand or may be assisted by computers.
[QUOTE]In Japanese "Hentai" refers strictly to sexual perversion. However in North America this word has been adopted and is used to refer to pornographic Anime/Manga.
That said, no, manga != porn. Manga is just as vast as anime or any other type of media (TV, movies, you name it).

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manga](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manga)
