### Post by bored2k on 2006-02-16
[QUOTE=Protostar]I LOVE Dragonball Z and Naruto. I'm tired of everyone hating on DBZ. I cannot understand why its so disliked by many.[/QUOTE]I think Dragonball was a little "too" overexposed. For good or bad (I think bad), thanks to anime like DBZ, the majority of the people out there who are not involved with/fans of anime but know it, automatically think that they shouldn't waste their time with anime as **everything** will be identical or highly similar to DBZ. You can't imagine the amount of people that go "Huh?!" everytime I mention a **non** shounen flick (shounen being the DBZ type) like Love Hina. My own **brother**, every he sees some action on my screen inmediately asks "What's the name of that anime? Is it like Dragonball?". So, I think that's the main reason why although a lot of fans like me (I'm refraining for using the word otaku on this post), although at a certain moment liked it (a lot), have grown to dislike it, specially when recommending anime to others (I don't think I have **ever** said: Hmm, you might like DBZ, go watch that).

On that note, although Naruto is without a doubt as shounen as an anime can get without having its main character named Goku and throwing kame-hames all over, I don't think in a few years it'll get as many negative comments as DBZ gets now that it's not the prime-time anime. Being so similar yet so different, old DBZ fans might like it because of the similarities, while DBZ haters might like it because it simply doesn't involve Goku in it.

---

### Post by Wolki on 2006-02-17
[QUOTE=bored2k]You can't imagine the amount of people that go "Huh?!" everytime I mention a **non** shounen flick (shounen being the DBZ type) like Love Hina.[/quote]

Now, it's hard to imagine a series that's more shounen than Love Hina. It ran in Shounen Mag, uses lots of Shounen themes, and seems like the biggest influence on the 'genre' for the last decade, for better or worse.

Shounen isn't the "DBZ Type", it's all series originally intended for boys up to around the early teens. Sports/Fighting is just a part of it.

I see your point though. I'd assume it's just the enormous popularity of DBZ, though, coupled with it's slowly moving pace in the anime. Anything that is popular will be hated, especially when it has obvious flaws.

---

### Post by bored2k on 2006-03-04
[QUOTE=psychicdragon]Is the Bleach anime any good or is it another Naruto?[/QUOTE]It is and it isn't. As you can see from reading Bleach manga, Bleach is arguably a lot more mature than Naruto. That said, although the episodes get goofy every once in a while (the comic face deformations are a **way** funnier than the manga ones), they're usually more sober and have a different feel to them. In terms of fill'o'rama, Bleach has been using a lot less filler episodes of what Naruto TV used when it had the same amount of episodes. Consequently, Bleach is as we speak embarking in a AFAIK 30+-episode filler arch. Overall, it's quite good. Although I haven't seen it since episode 45, the only reason to watch it would be for seeing the action sequences in full animation, because honestly, they stay **very** faithful to the manga, and watching them after reading the manga surely feels like reading it over again, with the difference of spending a huge 23 minutes for something you'd read in what, 5-10 minutes?


Man, it's been a **while** since I saw/read any manga...

---

### Post by psychicdragon on 2006-03-04
[QUOTE=bored2k]I disagree. If you only get excited with the current Naruto arch when Sasuke shows up, that's your thing, but although that's their ultimate goal, a lot of kick *** things have and are happening post the two year abscence. Naruto seems to alternate between being really amazing and complete shit. The last couple of chapters have been an improvement, but the entire Gaara rescue arch was bad IMO.

One Piece > Bleach & Naruto

*Setsuko and Seita are brother and sister living in wartime Japan. After their mother is killed in an air raid they find a temporary home with relatives. Having quarreled with their aunt they leave the city and make their home in an abandoned shelter. While their father's destiny who was a soldier is unknown the two must depend on each other to somehow keep a roof over their heads and food in their stomachs. When everything is in short supply, they gradually succumb to hunger and their only entertainment is the light of the fireflies.*

Probably the saddest movie I've ever seen. :(

### Post by Bragador on 2006-03-06
[QUOTE=Bandit]I am sorry I can not stand Anime. Maybe its becuase I am 28.
I really like Shrek 1 & 2 and Madagascar is great as well.
I can still get into a older batman cartoon if nothing else is on.
But that is about the extent of my cartoon stuff.[/QUOTE]

In Japan they use cartoons to make movies that are not always for kids while in north america and europe we tend to use cartoons for kids. This is why many don't have a good idea of what "animes" are.

One exemple of an old anime that opened the doors for the genre in north america was akira [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Akira_%28film%29](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Akira_%28film%29) . It's a science fiction story in which you can clearly see the influences of japan's past (especially the atomic bomb). You might want to try it. A more "modern" one is Macross Plus [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Macross_Plus](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Macross_Plus) . It's a little bit like a sci fi top gun movie.

Of course there are many other genres of anime since in japan, cartoons are not only used for "cartoons".

And if you want some comedy I strongly suggest golden boy. I don't want to spoil the fun but it's priceless. And if you are into computers you will most probably love épisode 1 [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Golden_Boy_%28anime%29](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Golden_Boy_%28anime%29)

---

### Post by Jucato on 2006-03-06
Love anime too :D Okay, maybe I'm an otaku (why would anyone want to be called a "geek", in english or in japanese? :D)
Anime was originally just the Japanese term for "animation". However, over the past years it has grown into a genre of its own. The most striking characteristic of anime would be the big eyes (and colorful hair). But anime also covers a great deal of other genres, from the childish to the adult to the purely for-adult-eyes-only :D I don't think it would be fair to categorize anime as just for children. But this isn't the place for that discussion :p

I think the mangaka probably changed his mind about what he wanted soul society to be like, so it left some loose ends. Now that the soul society arch is over, it doesn't really matter though.

### Post by bored2k on 2006-04-16
[QUOTE=fonggiding]I've got a general question. Why call it "Otaku" thread? I want to ask all of you what you think "Otaku" means. Please don't get me wrong. I am not trying to be challenging or anything. I just tend to hear that different people think that Otaku means different things. So other than the simple translation "nerd" (which most all of us Linux/Fourum people could be called) what is the general consenses on the definition?

Oh and I like One Piece(the comic not the anime).[/QUOTE]
> In English, otaku (plural as most Japanese words have no plural form) are a variety of geek (or an overly obsessed fanboy/fangirl) focused on anime and manga.

While in Japanese the term otaku has negative connotations, in English the term is more flexible; some fans believe it has positive connotations, while other fans believe it has negative connotations. Japanophile is a word sometimes used to describe an otaku. Wapanese is a derogatory term that is sometimes used, while others feel many cultures have their own equivalents.

Currently the term otaku is often used as self-description by anime fans with a minute and detailed knowledge. They use it to rally those who have recently begun to watch anime or read manga, and encourage questions on shows and Japanese culture in general.[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Otaku](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Otaku) ;)

### Post by qyot27 on 2006-04-16
> **fonggiding]I've got a general question. Why call it "Otaku" thread? I want to ask all of you what you think "Otaku" means. Please don't get me wrong. I am not trying to be challenging or anything. I just tend to hear that different people think that Otaku means different things. So other than the simple translation "nerd" (which most all of us Linux/Fourum people could be called) what is the general consenses on the definition?[/QUOTE]
For what it's worth, I would get thoroughly offended at the term if it was directed at me, but that's because I know that the usage in Japanese is disparaging and a fairly severe insult (the connotation is pretty close to pariah), and my behaviour doesn't line up with what that label implies, even in America - in other words, I prefer it, but I'm not obsessed with it in the least said:**
> I just watched Elfen Lied... wow. Good stuff, completely different from what I usually watch. The drawing was very well done, also, the story had the complexity that kept me watching the darn thing.
I watched it back in January, and it became an instant favorite.  The manga explains some things a bit more clearly, though, and it also plays out the whole story as opposed to the anime, which only took like the first half or a little over.  The manga has even more - and even weirder - fanservice than the show does as well.  I do like the character designs in the anime more than in the manga, though, at least at first.

### Post by psychicdragon on 2006-04-18
Wow, that's a pretty long list.

Has anyone else read the **Blade of the Immortal** manga? It hasn't finished yet, but there are 18 volumes released in Japan now. 15 have been published in English. I'm pretty much caught up with it now.

It's a samurai manga. The main character Manji is a former killer, who is granted immortality until he kills 1000 evil men. The plot is basically that Manji is hired by a girl as a bodyguard to help her get revenge on her parents killer.

What I really like about it is the artwork primarily. It's sort of a cross between traditional manga artwork and American comics style. It's also extremely gory, there are some really bloody battles in this one. I think JoJo is the only manga I've read where more blood is spilled. :rolleyes: It's definately not for kids, some of the chapters are pretty disturbing.

I attached a sample page to take a look at. I'd totally recommend Blade of the Immortal if you're looking for a manga to read.

---

### Post by Trisa on 2006-04-22
I don't watch as much anime as I used to, but it's still fun stuff to watch. My top favorite has to be *AIR*. Quite a wonderful series indeed. I've gotten the OST for it, along with some of the R2 DVD's, some doujinshi games and CD's, and the special edition of the movie. I guess you could say I'm a bit of an AIRhead, lol.:p

I've seen many series on television (too long to list), but here is what I currently have on DVD:

* AIR TV* [Vol.2 - Vol.3 and MEMORIES]
* AIR: The Motion Picture *[Special Edition]
*Alien 9* [Ultimate Collection]
*Azumanga Daioh* [All six volumes + Box]
*BASToF SYNDROME* [All six volumes]
*The Big O* [Season One Complete Collection]
*Black Jack* [Movie + OVA]
*Cowboy Bebop: The Movie*
*Fullmetal Alchemist* [Vol.1 - Vol.5]
*His and Her Circumstances* [Box Set]
*Last Exile* [All seven volumes]
*Metropolis*
*Neon Genesis Evangelion* [Platinum Vol.1 - Vol.3]
*Neon Genesis Evangelion - Death:Rebirth and The End of Evangelion* [Movie Box Set]
*Noir* [Complete Collection]
*Piano: The Melody of a Young Girl's Heart* [All three volumes]
*Pretear* [Complete Collection]
*Read or Die OVA*
*Serial Experiments Lain* [Box Set]
*Sister Princess* [All seven volumes + Box]
*Super GALS!* [All six volumes + Box]
*Witch Hunter Robin* [Complete Collection]
*The World of Narue* [DVD Collection]

Currently watching:
* Shinigami no Ballad* [Only two episodes to go, and the Shinigami is also my current avatar.]
* Monster* [Will start on this series soon. It's suppose to be really good.]

I wouldn't exactly call myself an "otaku", but I am a fan of anime.:-D

### Post by Trisa on 2006-04-23
[quote=delta32]How is it? I almost got this one before but decided not to.

I'm currently watching Suzumiya Haruhi no Yuuutsu ([http://www.haruhi.tv/)]("http://www.haruhi.tv/%29"). Greatest thing I've seen so far this season. :D[/quote] 
It's a pretty good series. I usually watch and enjoy series that last 26 or less episodes (there are exceptions, of course). Each episode is a stand-alone story, so there's no main plot in the show. However, it is still pretty good. Just as a forewarning, though; be sure to watch more than one episode if you plan to see it. The first episode can make you think that there's a forumla following the other episodes, but it really isn't. My favorite episode so far is episode four. You can also find what other people think [at the Animesuki Forums.]("http://forums.animesuki.com/showthread.php?t=26412")

By the way, it's nice to see that there are other AIR fans here at the forums. :-D
[]("http://forums.animesuki.com/showthread.php?t=26412")

### Post by bored2k on 2006-05-17
[QUOTE=wishyjr]hey guys, seen a cool looking anime advertised in a magazine i was reading called 'She: the ultimate weapon' anyone seen it? is it any good?[/QUOTE]
Although the English name might trick a few us (we all know it by SaiKano), My Girlfriend is actually a fairly popular anime (or at least we could say it has a fairly large cult following), and for a reason. I'd recommend you read a few reviews like the almost-always-great [ANN one]("http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/reviews/display.php?id=612")s, but from what I've seen, mostly everyone would recommend it. One thing though, if you're not into anime, I'm not sure you'd be able to appreciate its "Boy meets girl. Boy falls in love. Girl is a weapon of mass destruction." storyline without going "Where's my puking/breathing bag?" a few times..

 [http://www.madman.com.au/saikano/index2.html](http://www.madman.com.au/saikano/index2.html)

### Post by psychicdragon on 2006-06-02
I haven't read the Bleach manga in a couple weeks... The last chapter I read was when Ichigo had to fight Zangetsu again to unlock the power to control his Hollow side or something... I'd say that the characters (I guess Ichigo mostly) have gotten way to strong in way too short a time. Yes, it is a bit silly. 

Ichigo went from being just some kid to as strong as the several of the most powerful characters in a very short time. He only trained for about 2 weeks before they went to Soul Society right? Now they all have to get Even More Powerful™ because there are new Ultimate Powerfull villains™ that showed up. Even Goku and the gang took things slower than Bleach characters.

I think I liked the period in the beginning of the series when Ichigo had just recieved his powers the best. When he was going to school and fighting Hollows with Rukia's help. Now they're just spending all their time on the level-up treadmill.

---

### Post by Agent86 on 2006-06-20
[QUOTE=Wolki]Best show I've seen in years.[/QUOTE]
Ouran High School Host Club is terrific. I prefer Haruhi Suzumiya's *show*, but I like the Haruhi Fujioka *character* more.

I often don't know whether I'm responding to the random gayness or the random straightness of the show, but every ep has at least one laugh-out-loud moment, and usually more.

### Post by Horizon on 2006-07-03
[QUOTE=EdThaSlayer]so...any of you have seen Tokko?

that anime is pretty cool...has to do with alchemists and demons...[/QUOTE]

Read the Tokko manga. The anime pails in comparison...
I got excited when I noticed there was going to be a Tokko anime but I nearly threw up after the first 2 minutes.

You guys haven't watched Gundam? I suggest you watch the ENTIRE series (minus Gundam Wing, yuck). It is literally the best series ever made. If I had to list the best 5 anime of all time the first three would be from the Gundam series, and the last two spots would be empty...
It's very rare to be able to rank good anime (I've seen a lot of anime, only about 10 at most fit into the "good anime" category) because good anime is just damn good...off the scales, you can't even begin to compare and rank them. Now even taking this into account, Gundam is the only series that I can confidently and honestly say is THE best anime...EVER. Go watch it...NOW

lol *clears throat* *calms down* with that out of the way, I was wondering if you guys are into OSTs. If you are, what are your favourites? I'd have to say my top two are the two Melody of Oblivion OSTs and the three Samurai Champloo OSTs. The Samurai Champloo ones are especially nice to listen to whilst working. I've never had such a strange feeling from music, it actually makes you WANT to do something productive/work.

---

### Post by Miki01 on 2006-07-04
[QUOTE=Horizon]You guys haven't watched Gundam? I suggest you watch the ENTIRE series (minus Gundam Wing, yuck). It is literally the best series ever made. If I had to list the best 5 anime of all time the first three would be from the Gundam series, and the last two spots would be empty...
It's very rare to be able to rank good anime (I've seen a lot of anime, only about 10 at most fit into the "good anime" category) because good anime is just damn good...off the scales, you can't even begin to compare and rank them. Now even taking this into account, Gundam is the only series that I can confidently and honestly say is THE best anime...EVER. Go watch it...NOW[/QUOTE]

Yes, I agree. Except for the minus Wing part. Every forum or whatever I read that's got some kind of Gundam-talk always bashes on Wing. What's so bad about Wing? For one thing, Wing introduced me to the Gundam universe. I remember my first Gundam toy... I got it for Christmas when I was about 8 or 9, it was a large toyish semi-model kit of Wing Gundam. It was almost as tall as my PC's case. The main structure was built, all you needed to put together was the skirt, leg thrusters, ankle cuffs, wings, buster rifle and shield. It even transformed into bird-mode like it did in the series. I loved it... Now, all I've got are a couple of G-Seed action figures on display on the shelf in my room. I also have 1/8 (or smaller, don't exactly know) PVC figures of Cagalli (my favorite character) and Lacus from G-Seed. I even have a Cagalli keychain XD. I'm thinking next on getting some Initial D model kits after I finish building my leftover Tamiya cars, and a 1/6 resin model of Belldandy I received from a "figure relocation program" from another hobby modeling site I'm from. 

Wow, that was a long (and probably useless) post about my boring life...

If anyone actually does want to take a look at my boring life, feel free to check out my blog. Pics and progress are a small scroll down on my models. I may <hint>may</hint> add more progress shots of my models. I just don't know how to get my webcam (yes... a pooey webcam) to work on Ubuntu... But thats later... BACK TO TOPIC!!!!11eleven

### Post by FPStanley on 2006-07-16
I think the, "big deal" about FMA is that it can be appreciated on many levels (similar to how NGE could be watched as a typical big robot fighting show or a Freudian case study). If you just want to watch FMA as a fantasy/comedy, you can. Also, if you just want to watch it from a social commentary aspect, you can (the Ishbal people were based off the Kurds in Northen Iraq). I mean, lets be honest, while Naruto, One Piece, etc might be enjoyable shows, there is not exactly a deep underlying message present in them (I'm sure some people would argue that there is).

### Post by qyot27 on 2006-07-20
> **YourSurrogateGod said:**
> What was so immature?
To quote from the actual post on YM's homepage rather than the transcription of the IRC conversation that precedes it:

> Seems our Utawarerumono release pissed yesy off.

I think that's a good enough reason to keep doing it.

And we're not gonna drop Utawarerumono because people flame us.
The dropping comment was pointed directly at the fact yesy dropped Demonbane, but that, coupled with the rest of it (and the fact that it's a speedsubbing group going after a non-speedsubbing group) just is rather off-putting.  I would assume the initial spark of that involves the fact that YM picked Utawarerumono up at episode 16, which is one or two episodes ahead of where the other fansub groups are (and when there are already-said fansub groups which do have regular releases of the series existing, it's almost as if they are *trying* to start something).

Granted, some of that IRC conversation doesn't reflect too well on yesy either, but there's a difference between doing something just to piss other people off and keeping up a friendly rivalry (which is the deal between yesy and SS).

### Post by YourSurrogateGod on 2006-07-22
Has anyone watched "Beyond the Clouds"? That anime is incredible. The story is great and the animation is off the wall (think "Voices of a Distant Star", except slightly more detail.) It sort of reminds me of "Voices of a Distant Star", but with a twist. It's incredible. However, it doesn't approach the coolness factor of "Voices of a Distant Star" (although it's not far behind.) This movie was made by a corporation, whrease "Voices of a Distant Star" was done by a guy on a Mac. So... But if you have a chance, watch it. It's a touching/sappy story, but I'm kind of fond of those ^_^.

### Post by qyot27 on 2006-08-06
> **starscalling said:**
> lawl
i got a couple TB of the stuff.....
btw anyone know their way around x264 ish stuff?
im gonna try compiling mplayer as per the howto's b/c one of the groups i distro for came out with something thats so badass my system 'cant' play it ~____________~
To be completely honest, I don't know how fast libavcodec has gotten at decoding H.264 (judging off ffdshow, it's a lot faster than it was a few months ago, but I haven't played with it extensively).  For H.264 content CoreAVC beats it into the ground, but the downside to that is that CoreAVC is 1) Windows-only and 2) Proprietary and for-pay, although there is an extremely early version floating around online in various places that still blows libavcodec out of the water in regards to speed (it can play 480p content at fullspeed on a 1GHz Celeron with 256MBs of RAM and a 100MHz FSB, although maybe part of that is because I have my Windows setup very well-maintained and at arguably it's most basic level).  I don't know if CoreAVC would play nicely with WINE, I haven't tried.  I tried compiling mplayer once in the past and ended up failing miserably, so I just wait for the repositories to get updated.

I've actually switched to encoding my AMVs in H.264 for distribution, but that doesn't really pertain to this question.

### Post by NerdyShinobi on 2006-08-13
> **qyot27 said:**
> I've actually switched to encoding my AMVs in H.264 for distribution, but that doesn't really pertain to this question.

No, but it MIGHT just answer mine.  First of all, I appologize if this question has been answered previously in this thread or elsewhere.  My search-fu is not the strongest.

On the Windows side, I became addicted to using RealAnime to convert episodic TV to RVMB or x264, fitting on average 4 to 6 episodes on a standard cd. While OGMRip handles my dvd-rip chores like a charm, I have yet to find anything to help me handle reencoding .avi files to a more compressed format.  Do any of you guys recompress your fansubs to save space, and if you do, how?  mencoder command line black magick?  Avidemux-fu?  I figured if anyone would know, it's you guys.

And yes, I do plan to read all 180-some-odd pages of this thread, because I have TONS to catch up on.  I was out of the anime game for yeas, but a certain orange-suited ninja cadet sucked me back in about a year ago.  Insert Anime Bandwagon joke here if you want, but that giant frog was just to effing cool. :)

---

### Post by qyot27 on 2006-08-15
> **NerdyShinobi said:**
> No, but it MIGHT just answer mine.  First of all, I appologize if this question has been answered previously in this thread or elsewhere.  My search-fu is not the strongest.

On the Windows side, I became addicted to using RealAnime to convert episodic TV to RVMB or x264, fitting on average 4 to 6 episodes on a standard cd. While OGMRip handles my dvd-rip chores like a charm, I have yet to find anything to help me handle reencoding .avi files to a more compressed format.  Do any of you guys recompress your fansubs to save space, and if you do, how?  mencoder command line black magick?  Avidemux-fu?  I figured if anyone would know, it's you guys.

And yes, I do plan to read all 180-some-odd pages of this thread, because I have TONS to catch up on.  I was out of the anime game for yeas, but a certain orange-suited ninja cadet sucked me back in about a year ago.  Insert Anime Bandwagon joke here if you want, but that giant frog was just to effing cool. :)
Actually, I do all my encoding in Windows because that's where MeGUI, VirtualDubMod, and AviSynth are currently situated (although AviSynth is getting a Linux port and MeGUI running natively on Linux is just a matter of getting it to compile with Mono, I think it is - I think running it under WINE fails or something; I've never tried, but that's just what I've heard...of course, I also remember hearing that it couldn't compile with Mono yet, either).  That's not to say there aren't H.264-encoding solutions for Linux, though.  There's a GTK frontend, and there's also the straight commandline for x264 itself (if the syntax is the same as the Windows version, you could feasibly fiddle around in MeGUI to get the settings you like, copy the commands to a text file, and then call that into play when using x264 under Linux.

But no, I've never reencoded any fansubs, or at least the video stream in fansubs - the most I've ever done was reencode the audio in RAWs (and well, the old Tsubasa fansubs I'd packed away)  from MP3 to low-bitrate Vorbis and repack them into the Matroska container (i.e. MKV) to conserve that bit of space - I didn't know about avi_tc at that point so I couldn't remove the nasty nullframes and make them truly Variable Frame Rate (the ones which were 120fps, anyway - those null frames can take up 10-20 megs sometimes).

### Post by qyot27 on 2006-10-10
Ok, so what are everybody's lists for must-watch shows this season?  Maybe this can inspire a bit more activity for a little while.

Here's mine (links go to Anime News Network Encyclopedia entries):
[Galaxy Angel II](http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=6255) (or Ange-lune if you prefer, but I highly doubt I'll ever seriously call it that) (&#12462;&#12515;&#12521;&#12463;&#12471;&#12540;&#12456;&#12531;&#12472;&#12455;&#12427;&#65374;&#12435;)
[Asatte no Houkou](http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=6547) (&#12354;&#12373;&#12387;&#12390;&#12398;&#26041;&#21521;&#12290;)
[Kanon 2006](http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=6431)
[Yoake Mae Yori Ruri Iro Na -Crescent Love-](http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=6551) (&#22812;&#26126;&#12369;&#21069;&#12424;&#12426;&#29792;&#29827;&#33394;&#12394; -Crescent Love-)
[Bakumatsu Kikansetsu Irohanihoheto](http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=6819) (&#24149;&#26411;&#27231;&#38306;&#35500; &#12356;&#12429;&#12399;&#12395;&#12411;&#12408;&#12392;)
[Super Robot Wars Original Generation ~Divine Wars~](http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=6535) (&#12473;&#12540;&#12497;&#12540;&#12525;&#12508;&#12483;&#12488;&#22823;&#25126;OG &#65374;&#12487;&#12451;&#12496;&#12452;&#12531;&#12539;&#12454;&#12457;&#12540;&#12474;&#65374;)
[Happiness!](http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=6800) (&#12399;&#12404;&#12397;&#12377;&#65281;)
[Negima!?](http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=6708) (&#12493;&#12462;&#12414;!?)
[Tokimeki Memorial ~Only Love~](http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=6825) (&#12392;&#12365;&#12417;&#12365;&#12513;&#12514;&#12522;&#12450;&#12523; ~Only Love~)
[Otome wa Onee-sama ni Koi Shiteru](http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php?id=6619) (&#20057;&#22899;&#12399;&#12362;&#22985;&#12373;&#12414;&#12395;&#24651;&#12375;&#12390;&#12427;)

So, let's see, fanservice & comedy series, series based on bishoujo games, H-games, video games in general, what look to be sappy romance series, and remakes of Kanon and Negima (and yes, I know many of those overlap).  And with the apparent lack of fansubbers on GAII at the moment, I'm halfway considering assembling a group of my own, but I hope it doesn't come to that as I don't think I have the stamina for it - especially since I'd probably want to do a dual release XviD and H.264, but it's grueling waiting for H.264 to encode on my setup with the settings I use, especially for something on the level of a 24-minute show.  I'm also still not caught up on most series I started following in and since early 2005, with few exceptions, little less have the ability to deal with working on new-run series every week and not fall behind (as I'd likely have to be in charge of proofing, encoding, QC'ing - which is near-mandatory for the one doing the encode, and who knows what else).

> **Mihkal said:**

 which is based on an h-game. ](*,) 
Well, Kyoto Animation picking up Kanon was almost a sure bet after the success they had with Air.  I just wonder how many of Key's games they're going to end up animating.

Even though I doubt it, it'd be nice if they did an adaptation of Peace@Pieces, but that's wishful thinking.  I have seen other people say that though, so I don't know. I just really wonder what's going to be on the last Haruhi DVD.  Episode 00/11 was sold by itself on a single disc, and the rest of the DVDs sofar have only had 2 episodes each, which means that the only way to fit all 14 episodes in a 7-disc set would be to put three episodes on one disc.  The other alternative is that, if every disc continues to have 2 episodes on it, that the disc containing episode 14 will also hold an OVA episode.

Let see, my most beloved anime series:
1) The Twelve Kingdoms
2) Haibane Renmei
3) Kino's Journey
4) Crest of the Stars/Banner of the Stars
5) Berserk

Quite a rather mixed bag of genres, no?  :)

Other series I've really enjoyed (in no particular order):
Vision of Escaflowne
Planetes
Now and Then, Here and there
Rose of Versailles
Serial Experiments Lain
TEXNOLYZE
Neon Genesis Evangelion
Full Moon wo Sagashite
Cowboy Bebop
Full Metal Alchemist
Ranma 1/2

Well, this could keep going.  Then there's the list of stuff that I just liked and can recommend to others that like those genres.

In terms of movie animes, I'm a huge.. nay... HUGE Matoko Shinkai fanboy.  I can't get enough of his stuff.  I also really like the work of Satoshi Kon (especially his *Millenium Actress*), and I generally enjoy any Ghibli studios film.

### Post by qyot27 on 2007-01-27
As it turns out, the final Haruhi disc doesn't have an OVA on it - it's just the 14th episode and only that.  In the special features, however, there is a *full* 1½-minute version of the dance sequence which looks to me that the entire thing was reanimated (the familiar parts have a stark contrast to the way the same scenes looked and felt in the actual ED, so I'm assuming that it was completely reanimated rather than have new parts inserted into it - not to mention that in a couple areas, that would have been impossible or at least very hard, and the colors aren't as dark as they are normally).

### Post by Agent86 on 2007-02-14
Found a couple of interesting series this season, to replace Soukou no Strain and (guilty pleasure) Chokotto Sister that are ending their runs.

Nodame Cantabile is a neat little music-students-falling-in-love(???) show. This was a J-drama before becoming an anime, which seems like a reversal of the usual trend. Strong first ep. If it matters, voice actress Kawasumi Ayako (Mahoro, Aoi, Saber, etc.) is the lead in this show.

Gakuen Utopia Manabi Straight is a cheery show that belies its vaguely depressing premise of schools shrinking due to declining birthrates.

I really should catch up with Death Note even though I'm seeing mixed reviews on it. Nothing this season quite as good as Kanon 2006 (I guess I'm a KyoAni fanboy) or as surprising as Soukou no Strain (which is the real Death Note, if you go by body count), but Nodame and Manabi look like they'll be good shows.

> **qyot27 said:**
> In the special features, however, there is a *full* 1½-minute version of the dance sequence which looks to me that the entire thing was reanimatedYeah, it's a pretty cool sequence, and of course people are already doing the new dance and youtubing their efforts. I love how Haruhi has to guide Yuki around to get her back to the right spot :D  > **NerdyShinobi said:**
> Do any of you guys recompress your fansubs to save space, and if you do, how?  mencoder command line black magick?  Avidemux-fu?  I figured if anyone would know, it's you guys.Yeah this reply is extremely late, but... I don't recompress .avis to save space, but I do re-encodes to burn them into a format that can be played on my standalone DVD player (a Phillips DVP642-37). I used to use a combination of mencoder and Avidemux to do this, but lately I've been using only mencoder and getting reasonable results. I run a little bash script to do 2-pass encodes like so:
```
#!/bin/bash
#transcoding toobig.avi to transcode.avi
#1st Pass
#using xvid encoding and divx profile for standalone compatibility
#chroma_opt and trellis options help encode quality without impacting
#encoding time too much
#if you don't care about standalone player compatibility,
#remove the profile=dxnhtntsc option from both lines
mencoder -quiet toobig.avi -o /dev/null -ovc xvid -xvidencopts profile=dxnhtntsc:chroma_opt:trellis:pass=1 -oac copy
#2nd Pass
#if you're doing the transcode for archival purposes, and are willing to
#endure longer encode times, you should add vhq=4:bvhq=1:max_bframes=2
#to the option string in the 2nd line
#the bitrate=-170000 results in a filesize of about 170mb,
#so play around with that option to get your desired filesize
#the audio will be encoded as constant bit rate 128kbps mp3
mencoder -quiet toobig.avi -o transcode.avi -ovc xvid -xvidencopts profile=dxnhtntsc:chroma_opt:trellis:bitrate=-170000:pass=2 -oac mp3lame -lameopts cbr:br=128
```If you want the best quality/filesize, you probably want to transcode using x264 instead of xvid. I can't help you with that, but there's some info in the docs at the mplayer website.

### Post by qyot27 on 2007-06-15
> **Engnome said:**
> do you guys keep a list of series you've watched as well?
If I did it would be quite long.  I don't, though, and even going off the top of my head it would be rather hard to name all of them.  I might try to in a later post, though.

> Here is my "to check out list" anyway:
...
Mahoromatic
...
Rahxephon
I loved Mahoromatic (as is probably pretty obvious from my avatar), and I enjoyed RahXephon quite a bit as well.

> Some of them is pretty old but I add stuff when someone says "I liked that series" and not "there's a new series coming, looks nice so far" Not that age matters in anime, it's amazing how easy it is to find anime even if it's old.
I actually have a rather large disdain for the type of 'fans' that can't seem to see beyond the recent popular shows and/or Adult Swim - namely because I've been watching anime long enough to realize those aren't the end-all be-all of Japanese animation, not to mention that there have been gloriously few things I've felt at all compelled to watch that they've aired, over the course of their entire existence.

Heck, I really wonder how many of them would get aneurysms and complain that the shows were dull if Cartoon Network went back to showing Robotech or Tenchi Muyo at peak hours of the day like they used to back in the late 90s.  Or better yet, started showing either the recent re-release of Gatchaman or dug into their pre-Toonami - i.e. pre-St. Patrick's Day 1997 - archives and put G-Force back in rotation at a decent time (not like they do now with showing Gigantor and Voltron at dead hours of the night).

Sorry about that, I understand you, mainstream really sucks in some areas, it's just that sometimes it feels as if some people need to be different just to be different which always strikes me as immature. But that's mainly clothing anyway...

It feels like "you don't know anything!" Not that I claim to be expert or anything but you get the feeling shows like those are just to make money for some big company. No creativity or originality at all.
It's not that I dislike 'mainstream' anime, because I might actually like X or Y mainstream show once in a while - I just don't like the way they get overhyped by the rabid fanboys/girls and the type that you described in that last paragraph.  Despite posting in this thread, having watched/currently watching all those series, and editing AMVs in my spare time, I would actually get rather offended by being labeled an otaku, especially when it means getting grouped in with the fans that seem to just act as annoying as possible for no good reason at all.  In all seriousness, I really do just simply view anime as another form of entertainment - nothing more, nothing less (although part of the reason I like it, aside from the artwork, is that it's generally more interesting than the crap on American TV).

And I actually did come up with a list of stuff I have seen, although it might be missing some entries - I had to try and recall it from memory:

Not totally completed (not including series like X TV, Rurouni Kenshin, Comic Party, Saint Beast, Weiss Kreuz, R.O.D. The TV, and others I can't remember right now that I've only got one disc or just the first few episodes of):
[LIST]
[*]Dragonball Z (completely dropped it once Buu came in, but had been steadily losing interest since the Androids/Cell thing began)
[*]*Ranma ½ (I still need to watch seasons 3,4,5,7, and movies 1 & 3)
[*]*Robotech (just haven't seen the New Generation section - however, I did read all the novels; for that matter I've never watched Mospeada either)
[*]*Samurai Pizza Cats (I don't even know where I left off)
[*]Sailor Moon (not past episode 70 or 80-something)
[*]Voltron
[/LIST]

*am determined to finish, though

Completed:
[LIST]
[*]8-Man After
[*]Adieu Galaxy Express 999 (I can barely remember this movie, and I just recall being bored to tears)
[*]Ah! My Goddess OVA, Movie, and TV Seasons 1 & 2 (in that order)
[*]Avenger
[*]Azumanga Daioh
[*]Bokusatsu Tenchi Dokuro-chan
[*]Canvas OVA
[*]Cat Soup
[*]Chobits
[*]Chrono Crusade
[*]Elfen Lied
[*]Fatal Fury OVAs 1 & 2, Movie
[*]Final Fantasy 7: Last Order (count Advent Children if you like, but I don't like to call it 'anime' in the same sense as all the other shows)
[*]FLCL
[*]Fullmetal Alchemist, Movie, OVAs
[*]Galaxy Angel (including Z and A; I'm waiting to watch X until I see AA)
[*]Gall Force 1, 2, 3, Rhea Gall Force, Earth Chapter, and New Era
[*]Generator Gawl
[*]Gundam Wing
[*]Ichigo 100%
[*]Ikkitousen
[*]Inuyasha Movies 1-4 (I've never watched the TV series steadily - it's in the same boat as Cowboy Bebop and Ghost In The Shell: Stand Alone Complex)
[*]Itsudatte My Santa
[*]Jinki: Extend
[*]Kimi Ga Nozomu Eien
[*]Love Hina (including Specials and Love Hina Again)
[*]Macross II (Movie Edition)
[*]Macross Plus (OVA and Movie Editions)
[*]Macross Zero
[*]Mahou Sensei Negima (i.e. the first Negima series, not the one that started this past fall)
[*]Meltylancer
[*]Memories Off 1st, 2nd, 3.5, and #5
[*]Neon Genesis Evangelion, Movies (and for completeness, I've also read most of the Sadamoto manga, and also read the available chapters of the Iron Maiden 2nd/Angelic Days and Ikari Shinji Raising Project manga, and have completely read the Studio KIMIGABUCHI doujinshi RE-TAKE)
[*]Piano
[*]Please Teacher
[*]Please Twins
[*]RahXephon
[*]Rayearth OVA
[*]Rozen Maiden
[*]R.O.D. (Read Or Die) OVA
[*]School Rumble (both seasons), OVA
[*]Shamanic Princess
[*]Shingetsutan Tsukihime (played the game first, though)
[*]Street Fighter Alpha: The Movie
[*]Street Fighter II: The Animated Movie
[*]Teknoman/Tekkaman Blade
[*]Tekkaman Blade II OVA
[*]Tenchi Muyo! in Love
[*]The Melancholy of Haruhi Suzumiya
[*]X/1999
[/LIST]

Do not try to get me to list these chronologically; however, I can say that the first ones on that list that I watched were in the mid-90s - my anime watching didn't accelerate much until around 2002, though, when I started really actively looking for it.

A lot of my watching hit a snag after I started college, which happened around two and a half years ago.  That's when that huge queue started piling up, and when I would only rarely watch a show immediately after getting it (examples of which are Mahoromatic, Elfen Lied, Azumanga Daioh, School Rumble, RahXephon, and Galaxy Angel).  Except for Fullmetal Alchemist, the stuff I have watched off Adult Swim has been in such scattered fragments I can't really say that I've watched them (primarily talking about Cowboy Bebop and GitS:SAC here, and really in FMA's case, I watched the second half, movie, and OVAs via fansubs because I was impatient).

You actually get stuff like Cowboy Bebop and GitS on tv in the US? Lucky you! Oh and Pokemon of course since the game became a fad here. Sigh. It gives anime a very bad reputation here :( If they even know that it is anime, most people don't even know it is from Japan it's just another show.

Also, I have to say that I think Macross (Robotech in the US) is the worst anime I've seen, by far. It has a downright retarded story, unlikable and implausible characters, next to no character development, bad ending, bad everything. I'm sure the animation was cool back when it was new, though, and I'm guessing that's why it got popular.

### Post by qyot27 on 2007-06-16
> **Engnome said:**
> Wow that list must have taken a long time to compile from memory, interesting. I don't have my list chronologically sorted either. You find what you are looking for easier with a alphabetically sorted list. What would be interesting however would be a small X/5 rating after each show. ;) You haven't watched Berserk? Or is you list missing that one?
Well, a good number of the DVDs were sitting in front of me on my shelf, and I also ran through my stack of DVD-Rs with fansubs on them to jog it also.  And no, I actually haven't watched Berserk, although I've seen it used in AMVs numerous times.

> You actually get stuff like Cowboy Bebop and GitS on tv in the US? Lucky you! In Sweden the only anime we ever get is Swedish dubbed (barf) Sailor moon on the kids show at 18:00 (the kind of program that ends with the host saying "Goodnight all children, time to go to bed.") or some lame (also Swedish dubbed) card based fighting anime. Oh and Pokemon of course since the game became a fad here. Sigh. It gives anime a very bad reputation here :( If they even know that it is anime, most people don't even know it is from Japan it's just another show.
That seems like its about on-par with the state of anime on American TV around 1997 or 1998 when most things were heavily censored and shown during Saturday morning cartoon blocks.  That's not to say that still doesn't happen, but generally the ones that have that happen to them are already meant for a younger age group anyway - Pokémon, Yu-Gi-Oh!, etc.

Yeah, [_Cartoon Network's Adult Swim_](http://www.adultswim.com/index2.html) block shows them in different rotations (I'm not sure if Cowboy Bebop is currently in there, but it has been in the past and gets put back in at various times).  They don't play just anime though, they have original series and other things that get in there also, but in general it's a programming block intended for the ~15-25-year-old age bracket that goes from about 23:00 to 5:00 the following morning.  The schedules themselves: [_One_](http://www.adultswim.com/schedule/index.html) | [_Two_](http://www.adultswim.com/schedule/fix/index.html)

The Sci-Fi Channel also has started showing stuff again, although on Monday late nights (it was probably the first to widely show anime back in the mid-90s - again on Saturday mornings, although it wasn't heavily censored, possibly because it's a cable network).  ADV Films and Funimation also launched their own cable channels for showing anime, but those have a very small number of markets they're available in.  The one I'm in doesn't carry either of them, although there is a preview Video-on-Demand channel for Anime Network (ADV's station).

---

### Post by Agent86 on 2007-07-19
Let's see, what am I watching...

Sayonara Zetsubou Sensei is really dark comedy. It's also hilarious. Let's just say it's about two people who should have never, ever met. I watched it on a whim, because it was subbed by a.f.k., who also subbed Haruhi Suzumiya, Kanon '06, and Lucky Star. I hoped there might be something there, particularly since he picked it up in the middle of subbing Lucky Star - and I was right. A very pleasant surprise.

Zero no Tsukaima Season 2 is silly fun but the first season hooked me. A pink haired mage and her slave, I mean dog, I mean familiar.

Higurashi no Naku Koro Ni Season 2 is better animated than Season 1. If the story holds up, it should be a winner.

Potemayo is a whimsically odd little story of... erm.... beings that show up and cause havoc in your typical school situation. It's walking a tightrope between cute and magical, and doing a good job of it through 2 episodes so far.

Spring holdover Lucky Star is overall just OK. I'm hoping that the new characters from Ep. 14 rekindle my interest in the main show. But the Lucky Channel segment is pure win, and all that has kept me watching thru some dull spots in the main show.

My other holdover is Hayate the Combat Butler. It's a turn-off-your-brain, fourth-wall-breaking, occasional-cross-dressing harem comedy with a surprisingly strong central relationship.

---

### Post by Wolki on 2007-07-19
> **Agent86 said:**
> Sayonara Zetsubou Sensei is really dark comedy. It's also hilarious. Let's just say it's about two people who should have never, ever met. I watched it on a whim, because it was subbed by a.f.k., who also subbed Haruhi Suzumiya, Kanon '06, and Lucky Star. I hoped there might be something there, particularly since he picked it up in the middle of subbing Lucky Star - and I was right. A very pleasant surprise.

I've seen the first ep, and liked it so far. afk generally do great subbing jobs, but I don't like many of their series that much.

> Zero no Tsukaima Season 2 is silly fun but the first season hooked me. A pink haired mage and her slave, I mean dog, I mean familiar.


First season was ok, quite entertaining. Still waiting to see who will sub it to decide on a version, the ones i got the first season from apparently don't plan to sub it.

> Higurashi no Naku Koro Ni Season 2 is better animated than Season 1. If the story holds up, it should be a winner.


The first season definitely had its moments. The answer arc to the Shion/Mion thing was ... hard to describe, I don't think many series had that much of an impact since I read Narutaru Vol.6 years ago. Looking forward to this one. I like watching Higurashi in complete arcs, so I'll have to wait a little.

> Spring holdover Lucky Star is overall just OK. I'm hoping that the new characters from Ep. 14 rekindle my interest in the main show. But the Lucky Channel segment is pure win, and all that has kept me watching thru some dull spots in the main show.


Still stuck in the beginning... The first ep was painful, second one was a bit better, wasn't motivated to watch more yet. Maybe I won't at all. Lucky Channel was so far the best part.

> My other holdover is Hayate the Combat Butler. It's a turn-off-your-brain, fourth-wall-breaking, occasional-cross-dressing harem comedy with a surprisingly strong central relationship.

It is entertaining, no doubt. I like the narrator. :)

I have lots more holdovers, and many other things I didn't get to watch yet, so I'd be perfectly ok if this season gives me as few new things as January, and some time to cathc up before October.

Two new things I've seen:

Umisho: Haremy fanservice series. Was semi-entertaining. Will probably take another look, but as I like most of these series, it'll have to get better. The problem is, usually they start out interesting in the first few episodes, then rapidly get boring.

Zombie Loan: Action. One of the guys with a gun, the other with a sword. (surprise! what a great idea...) New Interpretations of a classic theme don't have to mean bad though. In this case, the pacing/storytelling seemed kind of messed up, so it's probably not worth it. Already have the second ep, so I'll watch that before I decide.

### Post by Agent86 on 2007-07-19
How could I have forgotten to mention the announcement of Haruhi Suzumiya Season 2 in this month's Newtype Magazine?  #-o No confirmed air date yet, but the best guesses are for Spring '08. Fall '07 is unlikely but still a possibility. As you can tell from my avatar, Taniguchi gives the news a \\:D/

> **Wolki said:**
> I've seen the first ep, and liked it so far. afk generally do great subbing jobs, but I don't like many of their series that much..I hope you grab Ep 2 of Zetsubou, it's even darker and nuttier than Ep 1. This show is well on it's way to becoming my favorite this year, let alone this season.

a.f.k is basically a one-man operation, with Strato doing the (excellent) translations, timings, encodes, etc. The other members of a.f.k. are QC and distro. Which is why I was surprised to see a.f.k. pick up a second show, especially since the very talky Lucky Star requires quite a bit of work to sub. Zetsubou, with all of the chalkboard scribbles needing translation, is a tough job, too - I don't see any other sub groups jumping aboard. SInce I'm a KyoAni fanboy, we'll have to agree to disagree on the quality of the series a.f.k. chooses to sub :)> **Wolki said:**
> First season was ok, quite entertaining. Still waiting to see who will sub it to decide on a version, the ones i got the first season from apparently don't plan to sub it.If you're talking about F-B, there's a hint on their forum that they may do ZnT S2 (aka Zero's Familiar - Knight of the Crescent Moons) after all. In the meantime, Kuroneko (who also did S1) are doing a decent job.> **Wolki said:**
> Looking forward to this one. I like watching Higurashi in complete arcs, so I'll have to wait a little.I wish I had your patience, to wait for the complete arcs. They're only going to do 3 arcs in 24 episodes, so your patience may be tested. Ep 1 is mainly a recap episode.> **Wolki said:**
> Still stuck in the beginning... The first ep was painful, second one was a bit better, wasn't motivated to watch more yet. Maybe I won't at all. Lucky Channel was so far the best part.Yeah, just FF to the Lucky Channel segments. I know that some compare this show to Azumanga Daioh (based on a 4-koma, group of high-school girls, slice-of-life, etc.), but for my money AzuDai is the superior show, by far. I think the main reason is that the AzuDai comic is so much better than Lucky Star's.> **Wolki said:**
> It is entertaining, no doubt. I like the narrator. :)Have you watched Gargoyle of the Yoshinagas? The voice actor that narrates Hayate plays the title role in Gargoyle, quite wonderfully. And it's an entertaining show to boot.

### Post by Wolki on 2007-07-20
> **Agent86 said:**
> How could I have forgotten to mention the announcement of Haruhi Suzumiya Season 2 in this month's Newtype Magazine?  #-o No confirmed air date yet, but the best guesses are for Spring '08. Fall '07 is unlikely but still a possibility. As you can tell from my avatar, Taniguchi gives the news a \\:D/

I must admit to not being a huge Haruhi fan, but I'm looking forward to it. :)

> I hope you grab Ep 2 of Zetsubou, it's even darker and nuttier than Ep 1. This show is well on it's way to becoming my favorite this year, let alone this season.


Ah, I already did the second I saw it out, but on that day my watching queue was already fulll. Saw it yesterday, and yes, it was awesome.

> a.f.k is basically a one-man operation, with Strato doing the (excellent) translations, timings, encodes, etc. The other members of a.f.k. are QC and distro.


Really, just one guy? Impressive. I knew they didn't have a lot of stuff from when they dropped AiBaby, but didn't know it was just one person... especially If you consider how fast the subs come out...

> If you're talking about F-B, there's a hint on their forum that they may do ZnT S2 (aka Zero's Familiar - Knight of the Crescent Moons) after all. In the meantime, Kuroneko (who also did S1) are doing a decent job.


Cool... I tend to like their work. I had plans for getting the Kuroneko one if noone but the current groups do it, so I'll wait a little and see what happens.

> I wish I had your patience, to wait for the complete arcs. They're only going to do 3 arcs in 24 episodes, so your patience may be tested. Ep 1 is mainly a recap episode.


Heh, I decided to get the Triad version for season 1 (until they dropped it). I've gotten used to being patient. :)

Only 3 arcs in 24 episodes? o_O Will have to see when I get the time to watch ~8 eps in a row...

> Yeah, just FF to the Lucky Channel segments. I know that some compare this show to Azumanga Daioh (based on a 4-koma, group of high-school girls, slice-of-life, etc.), but for my money AzuDai is the superior show, by far. I think the main reason is that the AzuDai comic is so much better than Lucky Star's.


Yes the Azumanga manga is great. I already thought the anime there lost a lot of steam after the first episodes... too cohesive for a 4-koma adaption maybe. Not that it was a bad series, it had its absolutely great moments.
My problem with LS so far is that it's mostly not that interesting or even funny. That "How to eat various things" segment in the first episode was almost unbearable and seemed to go on forever...

> 
Have you watched Gargoyle of the Yoshinagas? The voice actor that narrates Hayate plays the title role in Gargoyle, quite wonderfully. And it's an entertaining show to boot.

Not yel, I guess I'll check it out.

Manga - no comics
Anime - 85 episodes

[Naruto](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=roGbiSBkcKc)

[Bleach](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BmJQnHGvQrs)

Manga - 12 volumes
Anime - 37 episodes

[Ergo Proxy](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4oT2W3gaUBc)

### Post by Agent86 on 2008-05-19
Current shows I'm watching:

*Kurenai*. A bodyguard becomes the protector of a young girl. The bodyguard is a high school student. The 7-year old girl has never been outside her family estate. Drama, comedy, and slice-of-life ensue.

*Nijuu Mensou no Musume*. The world's greatest thief. A young heiress in danger. Get ready for adventure and entertainment. Unfortunately, the two groups subbing this show have chosen to use different English titles: *The Daughter of Twenty Faces*, and *Chiko, Heiress of the Phantom Thief*.

*Macross Frontier*. I'm watching this without previously seeing ANY Macross, and I'm enjoying it anyway. They give just enough background and history to keep me going without getting in the way of the story.

Those three shows are the cream of this season's crop for me. I'm also watching:

*Kanokon*: Unapologetic ecchi fanservice show. Ayako Kawasumi plays a fox goddess constantly coming on to the young schoolboy played by Mamiko Noto. This show has no point, and no shame.

*Itazura na Kiss* & *Special A*: High-school shoujo shows. They had rough beginnings but seem to have figured out where they're going. Neither is great, but they're watchable.

*Chi's Sweet Home* is running away with the Internationalization Award for this season. In addition to English, it's also being subbed in Spanish, Finnish, Russian, and Vietnamese. I'm sure it's being translated to Chinese, too, although I don't see those on TT.

---

### Post by Agent86 on 2008-07-21
Here's some of the Summer '08 shows I'm watching.

*Telepathy Shoujo Ran* - Nothing earth-shattering, but a pleasant enough story about a middle-school girl learning that she has psychic powers. Rather clumsily paced in spots, but it has appealing characters and an OP that's really grown on me.

*Yakushiji Ryouko no Kaiki Jikenbo* - aka Ryoko's Case File. Ultra-jazzy OP (and ED) sets the mood for the adventures of a *Thin Man*-ish pair of police detectives. Although this pairing has one very dominant partner (hint: look at the title). Modern setting with a retro feel, it's a lot of fun.

*Sekirei* - I guess this is my mindless fanservice show for this season. But spring's *Kanokon* has forever redefined the bar for fanservice (and mindlessness, for that matter). Powered fighting girls and harem comedy. It's OK but not compelling.

*Natsume Yuujinchou* - My pick of this season's shows. A young boy that can see spirits has to deal with a troublesome legacy from a powerful ancestor. And manjuu - lots and lots of manjuu. It has a lot of the Japanese emotion and sentiment that crosses the line into maudlin for some people. But I think it's well-balanced with a sense of menace and some great comic relief. It's not the fastest-paced show around, but you'll know after the first episode if it's for you or not.

### Post by qyot27 on 2009-06-11
Been quite some time since I last posted on this thread, but what the heck.

Currently I'm only following Bleach (through the arduous task of ripping the subs out of DB's releases, proofing them to my liking, and retiming them to the HD RAWs), and the travesty known as Rosario+Vampire Capu2 - honestly, I knew GONZO absolutely rapes storylines, but this is ridiculous, especially the inane censorship (and is it too much to ask to at least have *some* accurate points from the original manga plot?  The manga is pretty good, but the anime is utter crap).  And well, S2 of Haruhi, but considering there's only been one new episode so far...I'm still stuck at the beginning of volume 4 of the light novels, though.  Asu no Yoichi is another one that I've not started watching - I made sure to read all the available scanlated chapters of the manga first, just haven't gotten around to the anime.

However, I did watch Toradora! a couple months ago and instantly added that to my favs list.  I also watched the first ep of ToHeart2 adplus, but it was sort of meh.  A) The animation looks weird compared to the first OVA, although it's about the same as the ad OVA, and B) I want more Ikuno.

In other news, I've been trying to round up translators for some projects I want to see translated (and which have virtually nil chance otherwise).

And since MyAnimeList came up, my profile is here:
[http://myanimelist.net/profile/qyot27](http://myanimelist.net/profile/qyot27)



I'm also salivating over the prospect of a Blu-ray release of Kara no Kyoukai.  That and Rebuild of Evangelion 1.11 are at the top of my want-list on that front (although it doesn't really help that I don't even have a Blu-ray Disc player and the only BD-ROM in the house is the one on my mom's laptop).

### Post by JDShu on 2010-05-28
Otaku activities I recommend:

People should take a look at the Evil Series on youtube starting with [Daughter of Evil]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q46Osg9C4pA"). Its a series of songs made using vocaloids (think Hatsune Miku) and has a surprisingly good story. The order is Daughter of Evil -> Servant of Evil -> Regret Message -> Daughter of White -> Twilight Prank

[Katawa Shoujo]("http://katawa-shoujo.com/"), a visual novel based on Ren'Py also has a good story (though at first glance, the premise is a bit messed up) - plus it works on Linux :D
